'''Osaka's Three Great Summer Festivals''' (大阪三大夏祭り) are the three representative summer festivals held in [[Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[Osaka City|City]]: {{ill|Tenjin Festival|ja|天神祭}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Festival|ja|住吉祭}}, and {{ill|Aizen Festival|ja|愛染まつり}}.<ref name="Q426">[https://www.osaka-city-callcenter.jp/faq/detail.aspx?id=426 Q. What are the summer festivals in Osaka City?] Osaka City Comprehensive Call Center (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> In some cases, it refers to the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and {{ill|Ikutama Festival|ja|生國魂祭}}.<ref name="tennoji17">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000404/404034/tennoji17-07_p02.pdf Tennōji Ward Newsletter, No. 254, July 1, 2017] Tennōji Ward (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>
== Explanation of Each Festival ==
The three festivals consist of the Tenjin Festival of {{ill|Osaka Tenmangu Shrine|ja|大阪天満宮}} in [[Kita-ku, Osaka|Kita-ku]], the Sumiyoshi Festival of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}} in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], and the Aizen Festival at the {{ill|Shitennōji Temple|ja|四天王寺}} branch Aizen-dō (Araryōzan Shōman'in) in [[Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennōji-ku]]. These are sometimes collectively referred to as Osaka’s Three Great Summer Festivals.<ref name="Q426" /><ref name="osaka2">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000241/241118/15041934_2.pdf JR Momodani Station] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> They are also called Naniwa’s Three Great Summer Festivals.
Due to their common nicknames—Aizen-san, Tenjin-san, and Sumiyoshi-san—there is a saying, "Osaka’s summer festivals begin with Aizen-san and end with Sumiyoshi-san." This saying is thought to be the origin of the phrase "ai (愛, love) sumi (住, live) masen (ません, sorry)," or as a mnemonic, "ai (愛) sumi (住) masen (天, Tenjin)," re[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]]presenting the three festivals.
In some cases, the term includes the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and the Ikutama Festival of {{ill|Ikutama Shrine|ja|生國魂神社|1=Ikukunitama Shrine}} in Tennōji-ku, which is considered a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi" />
=== Tenjin Festival === The Tenjin Festival is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大祭り}}.<ref name="Q426" />
It typically begins with the eve of the festival on July 24, followed by the land procession and boat procession on the 25th. The boat procession features a grand fleet of 100 boats magnificently navigating the Okawa River.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Sumiyoshi Festival === The Sumiyoshi Festival usually begins with the eve festival on July 30, followed by the main festival and the Nagoshi Harae (Summer Purification Ritual) on the 31st, and concludes with the Mikoshi Procession on August 1.<ref name="Q426" /> The Nagoshi Harae is famous for the ritual of passing through a ring of straw (chinowa-kuguri) and is designated as an intangible cultural asset of Osaka Prefecture.<ref name="Q426" /> The Mikoshi Procession involves carrying the portable shrine from Sumiyoshi Taisha to Shukuin Tongū in Sakai City.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Aizen Festival === The Aizen Festival is held annually from June 30 to July 2 and is also known as the "Yukata Festival."<ref name="Q426" />
=== Ikutama Festival === The Ikutama Festival of Ikutama Shrine (held annually from July 11 to 12) is sometimes included. While the Tenjin Festival is referred to as the "River Tenjin" due to its boat procession, the Ikutama Festival is called the "Land Ikutama" and features a land procession, making it a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/chuo/cmsfiles/contents/0000508/508145/nabi2ura.pdf Connecting People, Cities, and History Through Festivals: Shinno-san and Ikutama-san Festivals and Traditions] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>

'''Osaka's Three Great Summer Festivals''' (大阪三大夏祭り) are the three representative summer festivals held in [[Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[Osaka City|City]]: {{ill|Tenjin Festival|ja|天神祭}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Festival|ja|住吉祭}}, and {{ill|Aizen Festival|ja|愛染まつり}}.<ref name="Q426">[https://www.osaka-city-callcenter.jp/faq/detail.aspx?id=426 Q. What are the summer festivals in Osaka City?] Osaka City Comprehensive Call Center (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> In some cases, it refers to the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and {{ill|Ikutama Festival|ja|生國魂祭}}.<ref name="tennoji17">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000404/404034/tennoji17-07_p02.pdf Tennōji Ward Newsletter, No. 254, July 1, 2017] Tennōji Ward (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>
== Explanation of Each Festival ==
The three festivals consist of the Tenjin Festival of {{ill|Osaka Tenmangu Shrine|ja|大阪天満宮}} in [[Kita-ku, Osaka|Kita-ku]], the Sumiyoshi Festival of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}} in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], and the Aizen Festival at the {{ill|Shitennōji Temple|ja|四天王寺}} branch Aizen-dō (Araryōzan Shōman'in) in [[Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennōji-ku]]. These are sometimes collectively referred to as Osaka’s Three Great Summer Festivals.<ref name="Q426" /><ref name="osaka2">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000241/241118/15041934_2.pdf JR Momodani Station] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> They are also called Naniwa’s Three Great Summer Festivals.
Due to their common nicknames—Aizen-san, Tenjin-san, and Sumiyoshi-san—there is a saying, "Osaka’s summer festivals begin with Aizen-san and end with Sumiyoshi-san." This saying is thought to be the origin of the phrase "ai (愛, love) sumi (住, live) masen (ません, sorry)," or as a mnemonic, "ai (愛) sumi (住) masen (天, Tenjin)," re[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]]presenting the three festivals.
In some cases, the term includes the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and the Ikutama Festival of {{ill|Ikutama Shrine|ja|生國魂神社|1=Ikukunitama Shrine}} in Tennōji-ku, which is considered a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi" />
=== Tenjin Festival === The Tenjin Festival is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大祭り}}.<ref name="Q426" />
It typically begins with the eve of the festival on July 24, followed by the land procession and boat procession on the 25th. The boat procession features a grand fleet of 100 boats magnificently navigating the Okawa River.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Sumiyoshi Festival === The Sumiyoshi Festival usually begins with the eve festival on July 30, followed by the main festival and the Nagoshi Harae (Summer Purification Ritual) on the 31st, and concludes with the Mikoshi Procession on August 1.<ref name="Q426" /> The Nagoshi Harae is famous for the ritual of passing through a ring of straw (chinowa-kuguri) and is designated as an intangible cultural asset of Osaka Prefecture.<ref name="Q426" /> The Mikoshi Procession involves carrying the portable shrine from Sumiyoshi Taisha to Shukuin Tongū in Sakai City.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Aizen Festival === The Aizen Festival is held annually from June 30 to July 2 and is also known as the "Yukata Festival."<ref name="Q426" />
=== Ikutama Festival === The Ikutama Festival of Ikutama Shrine (held annually from July 11 to 12) is sometimes included. While the Tenjin Festival is referred to as the "River Tenjin" due to its boat procession, the Ikutama Festival is called the "Land Ikutama" and features a land procession, making it a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/chuo/cmsfiles/contents/0000508/508145/nabi2ura.pdf Connecting People, Cities, and History Through Festivals: Shinno-san and Ikutama-san Festivals and Traditions] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>

revision.text:
'''Osaka's Three Great Summer Festivals''' (大阪三大夏祭り) are the three representative summer festivals held in [[Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[Osaka City|City]]: {{ill|Tenjin Festival|ja|天神祭}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Festival|ja|住吉祭}}, and {{ill|Aizen Festival|ja|愛染まつり}}.<ref name="Q426">[https://www.osaka-city-callcenter.jp/faq/detail.aspx?id=426 Q. What are the summer festivals in Osaka City?] Osaka City Comprehensive Call Center (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> In some cases, it refers to the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and {{ill|Ikutama Festival|ja|生國魂祭}}.<ref name="tennoji17">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000404/404034/tennoji17-07_p02.pdf Tennōji Ward Newsletter, No. 254, July 1, 2017] Tennōji Ward (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>
== Explanation of Each Festival ==
The three festivals consist of the Tenjin Festival of {{ill|Osaka Tenmangu Shrine|ja|大阪天満宮}} in [[Kita-ku, Osaka|Kita-ku]], the Sumiyoshi Festival of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}} in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], and the Aizen Festival at the {{ill|Shitennōji Temple|ja|四天王寺}} branch Aizen-dō (Araryōzan Shōman'in) in [[Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennōji-ku]]. These are sometimes collectively referred to as Osaka’s Three Great Summer Festivals.<ref name="Q426" /><ref name="osaka2">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000241/241118/15041934_2.pdf JR Momodani Station] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> They are also called Naniwa’s Three Great Summer Festivals.
Due to their common nicknames—Aizen-san, Tenjin-san, and Sumiyoshi-san—there is a saying, "Osaka’s summer festivals begin with Aizen-san and end with Sumiyoshi-san." This saying is thought to be the origin of the phrase "ai (愛, love) sumi (住, live) masen (ません, sorry)," or as a mnemonic, "ai (愛) sumi (住) masen (天, Tenjin)," re[[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 2]]presenting the three festivals.
In some cases, the term includes the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and the Ikutama Festival of {{ill|Ikutama Shrine|ja|生國魂神社|1=Ikukunitama Shrine}} in Tennōji-ku, which is considered a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi" />
=== Tenjin Festival === The Tenjin Festival is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大祭り}}.<ref name="Q426" />
It typically begins with the eve of the festival on July 24, followed by the land procession and boat procession on the 25th. The boat procession features a grand fleet of 100 boats magnificently navigating the Okawa River.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Sumiyoshi Festival === The Sumiyoshi Festival usually begins with the eve festival on July 30, followed by the main festival and the Nagoshi Harae (Summer Purification Ritual) on the 31st, and concludes with the Mikoshi Procession on August 1.<ref name="Q426" /> The Nagoshi Harae is famous for the ritual of passing through a ring of straw (chinowa-kuguri) and is designated as an intangible cultural asset of Osaka Prefecture.<ref name="Q426" /> The Mikoshi Procession involves carrying the portable shrine from Sumiyoshi Taisha to Shukuin Tongū in Sakai City.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Aizen Festival === The Aizen Festival is held annually from June 30 to July 2 and is also known as the "Yukata Festival."<ref name="Q426" />
=== Ikutama Festival === The Ikutama Festival of Ikutama Shrine (held annually from July 11 to 12) is sometimes included. While the Tenjin Festival is referred to as the "River Tenjin" due to its boat procession, the Ikutama Festival is called the "Land Ikutama" and features a land procession, making it a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/chuo/cmsfiles/contents/0000508/508145/nabi2ura.pdf Connecting People, Cities, and History Through Festivals: Shinno-san and Ikutama-san Festivals and Traditions] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

'''Osaka's Three Great Summer Festivals''' (大阪三大夏祭り) are the three representative summer festivals held in [[Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[Osaka City|City]]: {{ill|Tenjin Festival|ja|天神祭}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Festival|ja|住吉祭}}, and {{ill|Aizen Festival|ja|愛染まつり}}.<ref name="Q426">[https://www.osaka-city-callcenter.jp/faq/detail.aspx?id=426 Q. What are the summer festivals in Osaka City?] Osaka City Comprehensive Call Center (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> In some cases, it refers to the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and {{ill|Ikutama Festival|ja|生國魂祭}}.<ref name="tennoji17">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000404/404034/tennoji17-07_p02.pdf Tennōji Ward Newsletter, No. 254, July 1, 2017] Tennōji Ward (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>
== Explanation of Each Festival ==
The three festivals consist of the Tenjin Festival of {{ill|Osaka Tenmangu Shrine|ja|大阪天満宮}} in [[Kita-ku, Osaka|Kita-ku]], the Sumiyoshi Festival of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}} in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], and the Aizen Festival at the {{ill|Shitennōji Temple|ja|四天王寺}} branch Aizen-dō (Araryōzan Shōman'in) in [[Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennōji-ku]]. These are sometimes collectively referred to as Osaka’s Three Great Summer Festivals.<ref name="Q426" /><ref name="osaka2">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000241/241118/15041934_2.pdf JR Momodani Station] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> They are also called Naniwa’s Three Great Summer Festivals.
Due to their common nicknames—Aizen-san, Tenjin-san, and Sumiyoshi-san—there is a saying, "Osaka’s summer festivals begin with Aizen-san and end with Sumiyoshi-san." This saying is thought to be the origin of the phrase "ai (愛, love) sumi (住, live) masen (ません, sorry)," or as a mnemonic, "ai (愛) sumi (住) masen (天, Tenjin)," re[[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 2]]presenting the three festivals.
In some cases, the term includes the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and the Ikutama Festival of {{ill|Ikutama Shrine|ja|生國魂神社|1=Ikukunitama Shrine}} in Tennōji-ku, which is considered a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi" />
=== Tenjin Festival === The Tenjin Festival is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大祭り}}.<ref name="Q426" />
It typically begins with the eve of the festival on July 24, followed by the land procession and boat procession on the 25th. The boat procession features a grand fleet of 100 boats magnificently navigating the Okawa River.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Sumiyoshi Festival === The Sumiyoshi Festival usually begins with the eve festival on July 30, followed by the main festival and the Nagoshi Harae (Summer Purification Ritual) on the 31st, and concludes with the Mikoshi Procession on August 1.<ref name="Q426" /> The Nagoshi Harae is famous for the ritual of passing through a ring of straw (chinowa-kuguri) and is designated as an intangible cultural asset of Osaka Prefecture.<ref name="Q426" /> The Mikoshi Procession involves carrying the portable shrine from Sumiyoshi Taisha to Shukuin Tongū in Sakai City.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Aizen Festival === The Aizen Festival is held annually from June 30 to July 2 and is also known as the "Yukata Festival."<ref name="Q426" />
=== Ikutama Festival === The Ikutama Festival of Ikutama Shrine (held annually from July 11 to 12) is sometimes included. While the Tenjin Festival is referred to as the "River Tenjin" due to its boat procession, the Ikutama Festival is called the "Land Ikutama" and features a land procession, making it a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/chuo/cmsfiles/contents/0000508/508145/nabi2ura.pdf Connecting People, Cities, and History Through Festivals: Shinno-san and Ikutama-san Festivals and Traditions] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>

revision.text:
'''Osaka's Three Great Summer Festivals''' (大阪三大夏祭り) are the three representative summer festivals held in [[Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[Osaka City|City]]: {{ill|Tenjin Festival|ja|天神祭}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Festival|ja|住吉祭}}, and {{ill|Aizen Festival|ja|愛染まつり}}.<ref name="Q426">[https://www.osaka-city-callcenter.jp/faq/detail.aspx?id=426 Q. What are the summer festivals in Osaka City?] Osaka City Comprehensive Call Center (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> In some cases, it refers to the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and {{ill|Ikutama Festival|ja|生國魂祭}}.<ref name="tennoji17">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000404/404034/tennoji17-07_p02.pdf Tennōji Ward Newsletter, No. 254, July 1, 2017] Tennōji Ward (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>
== Explanation of Each Festival ==
The three festivals consist of the Tenjin Festival of {{ill|Osaka Tenmangu Shrine|ja|大阪天満宮}} in [[Kita-ku, Osaka|Kita-ku]], the Sumiyoshi Festival of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}} in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], and the Aizen Festival at the {{ill|Shitennōji Temple|ja|四天王寺}} branch Aizen-dō (Araryōzan Shōman'in) in [[Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennōji-ku]]. These are sometimes collectively referred to as Osaka’s Three Great Summer Festivals.<ref name="Q426" /><ref name="osaka2">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000241/241118/15041934_2.pdf JR Momodani Station] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> They are also called Naniwa’s Three Great Summer Festivals.
Due to their common nicknames—Aizen-san, Tenjin-san, and Sumiyoshi-san—there is a saying, "Osaka’s summer festivals begin with Aizen-san and end with Sumiyoshi-san." This saying is thought to be the origin of the phrase "ai (愛, love) sumi (住, live) masen (ません, sorry)," or as a mnemonic, "ai (愛) sumi (住) masen (天, Tenjin)," re[[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 2]]presenting the three festivals.
In some cases, the term includes the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and the Ikutama Festival of {{ill|Ikutama Shrine|ja|生國魂神社|1=Ikukunitama Shrine}} in Tennōji-ku, which is considered a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi" />
=== Tenjin Festival === The Tenjin Festival is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大祭り}}.<ref name="Q426" />
It typically begins with the eve of the festival on July 24, followed by the land procession and boat procession on the 25th. The boat procession features a grand fleet of 100 boats magnificently navigating the Okawa River.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Sumiyoshi Festival === The Sumiyoshi Festival usually begins with the eve festival on July 30, followed by the main festival and the Nagoshi Harae (Summer Purification Ritual) on the 31st, and concludes with the Mikoshi Procession on August 1.<ref name="Q426" /> The Nagoshi Harae is famous for the ritual of passing through a ring of straw (chinowa-kuguri) and is designated as an intangible cultural asset of Osaka Prefecture.<ref name="Q426" /> The Mikoshi Procession involves carrying the portable shrine from Sumiyoshi Taisha to Shukuin Tongū in Sakai City.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Aizen Festival === The Aizen Festival is held annually from June 30 to July 2 and is also known as the "Yukata Festival."<ref name="Q426" />
=== Ikutama Festival === The Ikutama Festival of Ikutama Shrine (held annually from July 11 to 12) is sometimes included. While the Tenjin Festival is referred to as the "River Tenjin" due to its boat procession, the Ikutama Festival is called the "Land Ikutama" and features a land procession, making it a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/chuo/cmsfiles/contents/0000508/508145/nabi2ura.pdf Connecting People, Cities, and History Through Festivals: Shinno-san and Ikutama-san Festivals and Traditions] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

'''Osaka's Three Great Summer Festivals''' (大阪三大夏祭り) are the three representative summer festivals held in [[Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[Osaka City|City]]: {{ill|Tenjin Festival|ja|天神祭}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Festival|ja|住吉祭}}, and {{ill|Aizen Festival|ja|愛染まつり}}.<ref name="Q426">[https://www.osaka-city-callcenter.jp/faq/detail.aspx?id=426 Q. What are the summer festivals in Osaka City?] Osaka City Comprehensive Call Center (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> In some cases, it refers to the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and {{ill|Ikutama Festival|ja|生國魂祭}}.<ref name="tennoji17">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000404/404034/tennoji17-07_p02.pdf Tennōji Ward Newsletter, No. 254, July 1, 2017] Tennōji Ward (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>
== Explanation of Each Festival ==
The three festivals consist of the Tenjin Festival of {{ill|Osaka Tenmangu Shrine|ja|大阪天満宮}} in [[Kita-ku, Osaka|Kita-ku]], the Sumiyoshi Festival of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}} in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], and the Aizen Festival at the {{ill|Shitennōji Temple|ja|四天王寺}} branch Aizen-dō (Araryōzan Shōman'in) in [[Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennōji-ku]]. These are sometimes collectively referred to as Osaka’s Three Great Summer Festivals.<ref name="Q426" /><ref name="osaka2">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000241/241118/15041934_2.pdf JR Momodani Station] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> They are also called Naniwa’s Three Great Summer Festivals.
Due to their common nicknames—Aizen-san, Tenjin-san, and Sumiyoshi-san—there is a saying, "Osaka’s summer festivals begin with Aizen-san and end with Sumiyoshi-san." This saying is thought to be the origin of the phrase "ai (愛, love) sumi (住, live) masen (ません, sorry)," or as a mnemonic, "ai (愛) sumi (住) masen (天, Tenjin)," re[[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category:tier 2]]presenting the three festivals.
In some cases, the term includes the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and the Ikutama Festival of {{ill|Ikutama Shrine|ja|生國魂神社|1=Ikukunitama Shrine}} in Tennōji-ku, which is considered a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi" />
=== Tenjin Festival === The Tenjin Festival is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大祭り}}.<ref name="Q426" />
It typically begins with the eve of the festival on July 24, followed by the land procession and boat procession on the 25th. The boat procession features a grand fleet of 100 boats magnificently navigating the Okawa River.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Sumiyoshi Festival === The Sumiyoshi Festival usually begins with the eve festival on July 30, followed by the main festival and the Nagoshi Harae (Summer Purification Ritual) on the 31st, and concludes with the Mikoshi Procession on August 1.<ref name="Q426" /> The Nagoshi Harae is famous for the ritual of passing through a ring of straw (chinowa-kuguri) and is designated as an intangible cultural asset of Osaka Prefecture.<ref name="Q426" /> The Mikoshi Procession involves carrying the portable shrine from Sumiyoshi Taisha to Shukuin Tongū in Sakai City.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Aizen Festival === The Aizen Festival is held annually from June 30 to July 2 and is also known as the "Yukata Festival."<ref name="Q426" />
=== Ikutama Festival === The Ikutama Festival of Ikutama Shrine (held annually from July 11 to 12) is sometimes included. While the Tenjin Festival is referred to as the "River Tenjin" due to its boat procession, the Ikutama Festival is called the "Land Ikutama" and features a land procession, making it a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/chuo/cmsfiles/contents/0000508/508145/nabi2ura.pdf Connecting People, Cities, and History Through Festivals: Shinno-san and Ikutama-san Festivals and Traditions] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>

'''Osaka's Three Great Summer Festivals''' (大阪三大夏祭り) are the three representative summer festivals held in [[Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[Osaka City|City]]: {{ill|Tenjin Festival|ja|天神祭}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Festival|ja|住吉祭}}, and {{ill|Aizen Festival|ja|愛染まつり}}.<ref name="Q426">[https://www.osaka-city-callcenter.jp/faq/detail.aspx?id=426 Q. What are the summer festivals in Osaka City?] Osaka City Comprehensive Call Center (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> In some cases, it refers to the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and {{ill|Ikutama Festival|ja|生國魂祭}}.<ref name="tennoji17">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000404/404034/tennoji17-07_p02.pdf Tennōji Ward Newsletter, No. 254, July 1, 2017] Tennōji Ward (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>
== Explanation of Each Festival ==
The three festivals consist of the Tenjin Festival of {{ill|Osaka Tenmangu Shrine|ja|大阪天満宮}} in [[Kita-ku, Osaka|Kita-ku]], the Sumiyoshi Festival of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}} in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], and the Aizen Festival at the {{ill|Shitennōji Temple|ja|四天王寺}} branch Aizen-dō (Araryōzan Shōman'in) in [[Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennōji-ku]]. These are sometimes collectively referred to as Osaka’s Three Great Summer Festivals.<ref name="Q426" /><ref name="osaka2">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000241/241118/15041934_2.pdf JR Momodani Station] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> They are also called Naniwa’s Three Great Summer Festivals.
Due to their common nicknames—Aizen-san, Tenjin-san, and Sumiyoshi-san—there is a saying, "Osaka’s summer festivals begin with Aizen-san and end with Sumiyoshi-san." This saying is thought to be the origin of the phrase "ai (愛, love) sumi (住, live) masen (ません, sorry)," or as a mnemonic, "ai (愛) sumi (住) masen (天, Tenjin)," re[[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 2]]presenting the three festivals.
In some cases, the term includes the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and the Ikutama Festival of {{ill|Ikutama Shrine|ja|生國魂神社|1=Ikukunitama Shrine}} in Tennōji-ku, which is considered a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi" />
=== Tenjin Festival === The Tenjin Festival is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大祭り}}.<ref name="Q426" />
It typically begins with the eve of the festival on July 24, followed by the land procession and boat procession on the 25th. The boat procession features a grand fleet of 100 boats magnificently navigating the Okawa River.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Sumiyoshi Festival === The Sumiyoshi Festival usually begins with the eve festival on July 30, followed by the main festival and the Nagoshi Harae (Summer Purification Ritual) on the 31st, and concludes with the Mikoshi Procession on August 1.<ref name="Q426" /> The Nagoshi Harae is famous for the ritual of passing through a ring of straw (chinowa-kuguri) and is designated as an intangible cultural asset of Osaka Prefecture.<ref name="Q426" /> The Mikoshi Procession involves carrying the portable shrine from Sumiyoshi Taisha to Shukuin Tongū in Sakai City.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Aizen Festival === The Aizen Festival is held annually from June 30 to July 2 and is also known as the "Yukata Festival."<ref name="Q426" />
=== Ikutama Festival === The Ikutama Festival of Ikutama Shrine (held annually from July 11 to 12) is sometimes included. While the Tenjin Festival is referred to as the "River Tenjin" due to its boat procession, the Ikutama Festival is called the "Land Ikutama" and features a land procession, making it a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/chuo/cmsfiles/contents/0000508/508145/nabi2ura.pdf Connecting People, Cities, and History Through Festivals: Shinno-san and Ikutama-san Festivals and Traditions] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>

revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
'''Osaka's Three Great Summer Festivals''' (大阪三大夏祭り) are the three representative summer festivals held in [[Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[Osaka City|City]]: {{ill|Tenjin Festival|ja|天神祭}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Festival|ja|住吉祭}}, and {{ill|Aizen Festival|ja|愛染まつり}}.<ref name="Q426">[https://www.osaka-city-callcenter.jp/faq/detail.aspx?id=426 Q. What are the summer festivals in Osaka City?] Osaka City Comprehensive Call Center (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> In some cases, it refers to the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and {{ill|Ikutama Festival|ja|生國魂祭}}.<ref name="tennoji17">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000404/404034/tennoji17-07_p02.pdf Tennōji Ward Newsletter, No. 254, July 1, 2017] Tennōji Ward (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>
== Explanation of Each Festival ==
The three festivals consist of the Tenjin Festival of {{ill|Osaka Tenmangu Shrine|ja|大阪天満宮}} in [[Kita-ku, Osaka|Kita-ku]], the Sumiyoshi Festival of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}} in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], and the Aizen Festival at the {{ill|Shitennōji Temple|ja|四天王寺}} branch Aizen-dō (Araryōzan Shōman'in) in [[Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennōji-ku]]. These are sometimes collectively referred to as Osaka’s Three Great Summer Festivals.<ref name="Q426" /><ref name="osaka2">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000241/241118/15041934_2.pdf JR Momodani Station] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> They are also called Naniwa’s Three Great Summer Festivals.
Due to their common nicknames—Aizen-san, Tenjin-san, and Sumiyoshi-san—there is a saying, "Osaka’s summer festivals begin with Aizen-san and end with Sumiyoshi-san." This saying is thought to be the origin of the phrase "ai (愛, love) sumi (住, live) masen (ません, sorry)," or as a mnemonic, "ai (愛) sumi (住) masen (天, Tenjin)," representing the three festivals.
In some cases, the term includes the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and the Ikutama Festival of {{ill|Ikutama Shrine|ja|生國魂神社|1=Ikukunitama Shrine}} in Tennōji-ku, which is considered a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi" />
=== Tenjin Festival === The Tenjin Festival is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大祭り}}.<ref name="Q426" />
It typically begins with the eve of the festival on July 24, followed by the land procession and boat procession on the 25th. The boat procession features a grand fleet of 100 boats magnificently navigating the Okawa River.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Sumiyoshi Festival === The Sumiyoshi Festival usually begins with the eve festival on July 30, followed by the main festival and the Nagoshi Harae (Summer Purification Ritual) on the 31st, and concludes with the Mikoshi Procession on August 1.<ref name="Q426" /> The Nagoshi Harae is famous for the ritual of passing through a ring of straw (chinowa-kuguri) and is designated as an intangible cultural asset of Osaka Prefecture.<ref name="Q426" /> The Mikoshi Procession involves carrying the portable shrine from Sumiyoshi Taisha to Shukuin Tongū in Sakai City.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Aizen Festival === The Aizen Festival is held annually from June 30 to July 2 and is also known as the "Yukata Festival."<ref name="Q426" />
=== Ikutama Festival === The Ikutama Festival of Ikutama Shrine (held annually from July 11 to 12) is sometimes included. While the Tenjin Festival is referred to as the "River Tenjin" due to its boat procession, the Ikutama Festival is called the "Land Ikutama" and features a land procession, making it a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/chuo/cmsfiles/contents/0000508/508145/nabi2ura.pdf Connecting People, Cities, and History Through Festivals: Shinno-san and Ikutama-san Festivals and Traditions] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Festival|ja|祭}} * {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}

* {{ill|Spring Festivals|en}}<!--Three Great Summer Festivals of Osaka--> * {{ill|Autumn Festivals|en}}<!--Three Great Summer Festivals of Osaka--> * {{ill|Winter Festivals|en}}<!--Three Great Summer {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Festivals in Osaka City]] [[Category:Three Great Festivals]] [[Category:Three Great Summer Festivals of Osaka]] [[Category:Japanese Culture]] [[Category:Festivals in Japan]] [[Category:Festivals]] [[Category:Summer]] [[Category:Seasonal Words for Summer]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Summer festival]] [[Category:11-01-18]] [[Category:Summer festivals forks]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:大阪三大夏祭り]] [[ja:兵庫県]] [[ja:湯村温泉 (兵庫県)#イベント]] [[ja:鳥取県]] [[ja:鳥取しゃんしゃん祭]] [[ja:徳島県]] [[ja:阿波踊り]] [[ja:徳島市阿波おどり]] [[ja:四国三大祭り]] [[ja:高知県]] [[ja:よさこい祭り]] [[ja:福岡県]] [[ja:博多祇園山笠]] [[ja:熊本県]] [[ja:山鹿灯籠まつり]] [[ja:宮崎県]] [[ja:都城六月灯おかげ祭り]] [[ja:サバントゥイ]] [[ja:ヴォルガ川]] [[ja:ロシア]] [[ja:春祭り]] [[ja:秋祭り]] [[ja:冬祭り]] [[ja:七夕]] [[ja:地域おこし]] [[ja:帰省]] [[ja:夏休み]] [[zh:%E5%A4%8F%E7%A5%AD]] [[ja:夏祭り]] [[sn:Three Great Summer Festivals of Osaka]] [[sn:hree Great Summer Festivals of Osaka]]

'''Osaka's Three Great Summer Festivals''' (大阪三大夏祭り) are the three representative summer festivals held in [[Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[Osaka City|City]]: {{ill|Tenjin Festival|ja|天神祭|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Festival|ja|住吉祭|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Aizen Festival|ja|愛染まつり|12=draft}}.<ref name="Q426">[https://www.osaka-city-callcenter.jp/faq/detail.aspx?id=426 Q. What are the summer festivals in Osaka City?] Osaka City Comprehensive Call Center (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> In some cases, it refers to the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and {{ill|Ikutama Festival|ja|生國魂祭|12=draft}}.<ref name="tennoji17">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000404/404034/tennoji17-07_p02.pdf Tennōji Ward Newsletter, No. 254, July 1, 2017] Tennōji Ward (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>
== Explanation of Each Festival ==
The three festivals consist of the Tenjin Festival of {{ill|Osaka Tenmangu Shrine|ja|大阪天満宮|12=draft}} in [[Kita-ku, Osaka|Kita-ku]], the Sumiyoshi Festival of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|12=draft}} in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], and the Aizen Festival at the {{ill|Shitennōji Temple|ja|四天王寺|12=draft}} branch Aizen-dō (Araryōzan Shōman'in) in [[Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennōji-ku]]. These are sometimes collectively referred to as Osaka’s Three Great Summer Festivals.<ref name="Q426" /><ref name="osaka2">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000241/241118/15041934_2.pdf JR Momodani Station] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> They are also called Naniwa’s Three Great Summer Festivals.
Due to their common nicknames—Aizen-san, Tenjin-san, and Sumiyoshi-san—there is a saying, "Osaka’s summer festivals begin with Aizen-san and end with Sumiyoshi-san." This saying is thought to be the origin of the phrase "ai (愛, love) sumi (住, live) masen (ません, sorry)," or as a mnemonic, "ai (愛) sumi (住) masen (天, Tenjin)," representing the three festivals.
In some cases, the term includes the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and the Ikutama Festival of {{ill|Ikutama Shrine|ja|生國魂神社|1=Ikukunitama Shrine|12=draft}} in Tennōji-ku, which is considered a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi" />
=== Tenjin Festival === The Tenjin Festival is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大祭り|12=draft}}.<ref name="Q426" />
It typically begins with the eve of the festival on July 24, followed by the land procession and boat procession on the 25th. The boat procession features a grand fleet of 100 boats magnificently navigating the Okawa River.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Sumiyoshi Festival === The Sumiyoshi Festival usually begins with the eve festival on July 30, followed by the main festival and the Nagoshi Harae (Summer Purification Ritual) on the 31st, and concludes with the Mikoshi Procession on August 1.<ref name="Q426" /> The Nagoshi Harae is famous for the ritual of passing through a ring of straw (chinowa-kuguri) and is designated as an intangible cultural asset of Osaka Prefecture.<ref name="Q426" /> The Mikoshi Procession involves carrying the portable shrine from Sumiyoshi Taisha to Shukuin Tongū in Sakai City.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Aizen Festival === The Aizen Festival is held annually from June 30 to July 2 and is also known as the "Yukata Festival."<ref name="Q426" />
=== Ikutama Festival === The Ikutama Festival of Ikutama Shrine (held annually from July 11 to 12) is sometimes included. While the Tenjin Festival is referred to as the "River Tenjin" due to its boat procession, the Ikutama Festival is called the "Land Ikutama" and features a land procession, making it a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/chuo/cmsfiles/contents/0000508/508145/nabi2ura.pdf Connecting People, Cities, and History Through Festivals: Shinno-san and Ikutama-san Festivals and Traditions] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>

'''Osaka's Three Great Summer Festivals''' (大阪三大夏祭り) are the three representative summer festivals held in [[Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[Osaka City|City]]: {{ill|Tenjin Festival|ja|天神祭|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Festival|ja|住吉祭|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Aizen Festival|ja|愛染まつり|12=draft}}.<ref name="Q426">[https://www.osaka-city-callcenter.jp/faq/detail.aspx?id=426 Q. What are the summer festivals in Osaka City?] Osaka City Comprehensive Call Center (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> In some cases, it refers to the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and {{ill|Ikutama Festival|ja|生國魂祭|12=draft}}.<ref name="tennoji17">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000404/404034/tennoji17-07_p02.pdf Tennōji Ward Newsletter, No. 254, July 1, 2017] Tennōji Ward (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>
== Explanation of Each Festival ==
The three festivals consist of the Tenjin Festival of {{ill|Osaka Tenmangu Shrine|ja|大阪天満宮|12=draft}} in [[Kita-ku, Osaka|Kita-ku]], the Sumiyoshi Festival of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|12=draft}} in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], and the Aizen Festival at the {{ill|Shitennōji Temple|ja|四天王寺|12=draft}} branch Aizen-dō (Araryōzan Shōman'in) in [[Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennōji-ku]]. These are sometimes collectively referred to as Osaka’s Three Great Summer Festivals.<ref name="Q426" /><ref name="osaka2">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000241/241118/15041934_2.pdf JR Momodani Station] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> They are also called Naniwa’s Three Great Summer Festivals.
Due to their common nicknames—Aizen-san, Tenjin-san, and Sumiyoshi-san—there is a saying, "Osaka’s summer festivals begin with Aizen-san and end with Sumiyoshi-san." This saying is thought to be the origin of the phrase "ai (愛, love) sumi (住, live) masen (ません, sorry)," or as a mnemonic, "ai (愛) sumi (住) masen (天, Tenjin)," representing the three festivals.
In some cases, the term includes the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and the Ikutama Festival of {{ill|Ikutama Shrine|ja|生國魂神社|1=Ikukunitama Shrine|12=draft}} in Tennōji-ku, which is considered a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi" />
=== Tenjin Festival === The Tenjin Festival is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大祭り|12=draft}}.<ref name="Q426" />
It typically begins with the eve of the festival on July 24, followed by the land procession and boat procession on the 25th. The boat procession features a grand fleet of 100 boats magnificently navigating the Okawa River.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Sumiyoshi Festival === The Sumiyoshi Festival usually begins with the eve festival on July 30, followed by the main festival and the Nagoshi Harae (Summer Purification Ritual) on the 31st, and concludes with the Mikoshi Procession on August 1.<ref name="Q426" /> The Nagoshi Harae is famous for the ritual of passing through a ring of straw (chinowa-kuguri) and is designated as an intangible cultural asset of Osaka Prefecture.<ref name="Q426" /> The Mikoshi Procession involves carrying the portable shrine from Sumiyoshi Taisha to Shukuin Tongū in Sakai City.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Aizen Festival === The Aizen Festival is held annually from June 30 to July 2 and is also known as the "Yukata Festival."<ref name="Q426" />
=== Ikutama Festival === The Ikutama Festival of Ikutama Shrine (held annually from July 11 to 12) is sometimes included. While the Tenjin Festival is referred to as the "River Tenjin" due to its boat procession, the Ikutama Festival is called the "Land Ikutama" and features a land procession, making it a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/chuo/cmsfiles/contents/0000508/508145/nabi2ura.pdf Connecting People, Cities, and History Through Festivals: Shinno-san and Ikutama-san Festivals and Traditions] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>

revision.text:
'''大阪三大夏祭り'''（おおさかさんだいなつまつり）は、[[大阪府]][[大阪市]]で開催される代表的な3つの夏祭り。[[天神祭]]、[[住吉祭]]、[[愛染まつり]]の3つの祭<ref name="Q426">[https://www.osaka-city-callcenter.jp/faq/detail.aspx?id=426 Q.大阪市の夏祭りを教えてください。] 大阪市総合コールセンター（2021年7月15日閲覧）</ref>または天神祭、住吉祭、[[生國魂祭]]の3つの祭をいう<ref name="tennoji17">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000404/404034/tennoji17-07_p02.pdf 天王寺区広報紙 平成29年（2017）7月1日 No.254号] 天王寺区（2021年7月15日閲覧）</ref>。
== 各祭の解説 == 大阪市[[北区 (大阪市)|北区]]の[[大阪天満宮]]の天神祭、大阪市[[住吉区]]の[[住吉大社]]の住吉祭、大阪市[[天王寺区]]の[[四天王寺]]支院愛染堂（荒陵山勝鬘院）の[[愛染まつり]]の3つの祭を大阪三大夏祭りということがある<ref name="Q426" /><ref name="osaka2">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000241/241118/15041934_2.pdf JR桃谷駅] 大阪市（2021年7月15日閲覧）</ref>。浪花三大夏祭とも呼ばれる。愛染さん、天神さん、住吉さんと愛称で呼ばれることが多いことから、「大阪の夏祭りは愛染さんで始まって住吉さんで終る」といわれ、「あい（=愛）すみ（=住）ません」という言葉の語源となったという説や、「あい（=愛）すみ（=住）ません（=天）」と、大阪三大夏祭りの3つの覚え方として知られる。
天神祭と住吉祭のほか、天神祭の対に当たる祭とされる大阪市天王寺区にある[[生國魂神社]]の生國魂祭の3つの祭をいう場合もある<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi" />。
=== 天神祭 === 天神祭は[[日本三大祭り]]の一つに挙げられる祭<ref name="Q426" />。
例年7月24日の宵宮で始まり、25日には陸渡御と船渡御が行われ、特に船渡御では100隻もの大船団が大川を華麗に航行する<ref name="Q426" />。
=== 住吉祭 === 住吉祭は例年7月30日の宵宮祭で始まり、31日には例大祭と夏越祓神事が行われ、8月1日には神輿渡御祭が行われる<ref name="Q426" />。夏越祓神事は茅の輪くぐりが有名で大阪府指定無形文化財<ref name="Q426" />。神輿渡御祭は住吉大社から堺市の宿院頓宮まで神輿渡御が行われる<ref name="Q426" />。
=== 愛染祭 === 愛染祭は例年6月30日から7月2日にかけて行われ「浴衣まつり」とも呼ばれている<ref name="Q426" />。
=== 生國魂祭 === 生國魂神社の生國魂祭（例年7月11日から12日）が挙げられることもあり、天神祭が「川の天神」と称され「船渡御」が行われるのに対し、生國魂祭は「陸の生國魂」と称され「陸渡御」が行われるなど天神祭と対になるお祭りだった<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/chuo/cmsfiles/contents/0000508/508145/nabi2ura.pdf 祭りがつなぐ、人・まち・歴史 新農さんと生國魂さんのお祭りと伝承] 大阪市（2021年7月15日閲覧）</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references/>
== 関連項目 == *[[祭]] *[[大阪市]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおさかさんたいなつまつり}} [[Category:大阪市の祭り|*]] [[Category:三大祭り]]

例年7月24日の宵宮で始まり、25日には陸渡御と船渡御が行われ、特に船渡御では100隻もの大船団が大川を華麗に航行する<ref name="Q426" />。

=== 愛染祭 === 愛染祭は例年6月30日から7月2日にかけて行われ「浴衣まつり」とも呼ばれている<ref name="Q426" />。
=== 生國魂祭 === 生國魂神社の生國魂祭（例年7月11日から12日）が挙げられることもあり、天神祭が「川の天神」と称され「船渡御」が行われるのに対し、生國魂祭は「陸の生國魂」と称され「陸渡御」が行われるなど天神祭と対になるお祭りだった<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/chuo/cmsfiles/contents/0000508/508145/nabi2ura.pdf 祭りがつなぐ、人・まち・歴史 新農さんと生國魂さんのお祭りと伝承] 大阪市（2021年7月15日閲覧）</ref>。

What are the summer festivals in Osaka City?] Osaka City Comprehensive Call Center (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> In some cases, it refers to the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and {{ill|Ikutama Festival|ja|生國魂祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="tennoji17">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000404/404034/tennoji17-07_p02.pdf Tennōji Ward Newsletter, No. 254, July 1, 2017] Tennōji Ward (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>
== Explanation of Each Festival ==
The three festivals consist of the Tenjin Festival of {{ill|Osaka Tenmangu Shrine|ja|大阪天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in [[Kita-ku, Osaka|Kita-ku]], the Sumiyoshi Festival of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], and the Aizen Festival at the {{ill|Shitennōji Temple|ja|四天王寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} branch Aizen-dō (Araryōzan Shōman'in) in [[Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennōji-ku]]. These are sometimes collectively referred to as Osaka’s Three Great Summer Festivals.<ref name="Q426" /><ref name="osaka2">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000241/241118/15041934_2.pdf JR Momodani Station] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> They are also called Naniwa’s Three Great Summer Festivals.
Due to their common nicknames—Aizen-san, Tenjin-san, and Sumiyoshi-san—there is a saying, "Osaka’s summer festivals begin with Aizen-san and end with Sumiyoshi-san." This saying is thought to be the origin of the phrase "ai (愛, love) sumi (住, live) masen (ません, sorry)," or as a mnemonic, "ai (愛) sumi (住) masen (天, Tenjin)," representing the three festivals.
In some cases, the term includes the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and the Ikutama Festival of {{ill|Ikutama Shrine|ja|生國魂神社|1=Ikukunitama Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Tennōji-ku, which is considered a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi" />
=== Tenjin Festival === The Tenjin Festival is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="Q426" />
It typically begins with the eve of the festival on July 24, followed by the land procession and boat procession on the 25th. The boat procession features a grand fleet of 100 boats magnificently navigating the Okawa River.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Sumiyoshi Festival === The Sumiyoshi Festival usually begins with the eve festival on July 30, followed by the main festival and the Nagoshi Harae (Summer Purification Ritual) on the 31st, and concludes with the Mikoshi Procession on August 1.<ref name="Q426" /> The Nagoshi Harae is famous for the ritual of passing through a ring of straw (chinowa-kuguri) and is designated as an intangible cultural asset of Osaka Prefecture.<ref name="Q426" /> The Mikoshi Procession involves carrying the portable shrine from Sumiyoshi Taisha to Shukuin Tongū in Sakai City.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Aizen Festival === The Aizen Festival is held annually from June 30 to July 2 and is also known as the "Yukata Festival."<ref name="Q426" />
=== Ikutama Festival === The Ikutama Festival of Ikutama Shrine (held annually from July 11 to 12) is sometimes included. While the Tenjin Festival is referred to as the "River Tenjin" due to its boat procession, the Ikutama Festival is called the "Land Ikutama" and features a land procession, making it a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/chuo/cmsfiles/contents/0000508/508145/nabi2ura.pdf Connecting People, Cities, and History Through Festivals: Shinno-san and Ikutama-san Festivals and Traditions] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>

'''Osaka's Three Great Summer Festivals''' (大阪三大夏祭り) are the three representative summer festivals held in [[Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[Osaka City|City]]: {{ill|Tenjin Festival|ja|天神祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Festival|ja|住吉祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Aizen Festival|ja|愛染まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="Q426">[https://www.osaka-city-callcenter.jp/faq/detail.aspx?id=426 Q. '''Osaka's Three Great Summer Festivals''' (大阪三大夏祭り) are the three representative summer festivals held in [[Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[Osaka City|City]]: {{ill|Tenjin Festival|ja|天神祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Festival|ja|住吉祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Aizen Festival|ja|愛染まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="Q426">[https://www.osaka-city-callcenter.jp/faq/detail.aspx?id=426 Q. What are the summer festivals in Osaka City?] Osaka City Comprehensive Call Center (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> In some cases, it refers to the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and {{ill|Ikutama Festival|ja|生國魂祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="tennoji17">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000404/404034/tennoji17-07_p02.pdf Tennōji Ward Newsletter, No. 254, July 1, 2017] Tennōji Ward (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>
== Explanation of Each Festival ==
The three festivals consist of the Tenjin Festival of {{ill|Osaka Tenmangu Shrine|ja|大阪天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in [[Kita-ku, Osaka|Kita-ku]], the Sumiyoshi Festival of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], and the Aizen Festival at the {{ill|Shitennōji Temple|ja|四天王寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} branch Aizen-dō (Araryōzan Shōman'in) in [[Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennōji-ku]]. These are sometimes collectively referred to as Osaka’s Three Great Summer Festivals.<ref name="Q426" /><ref name="osaka2">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000241/241118/15041934_2.pdf JR Momodani Station] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> They are also called Naniwa’s Three Great Summer Festivals.
Due to their common nicknames—Aizen-san, Tenjin-san, and Sumiyoshi-san—there is a saying, "Osaka’s summer festivals begin with Aizen-san and end with Sumiyoshi-san." This saying is thought to be the origin of the phrase "ai (愛, love) sumi (住, live) masen (ません, sorry)," or as a mnemonic, "ai (愛) sumi (住) masen (天, Tenjin)," representing the three festivals.
In some cases, the term includes the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and the Ikutama Festival of {{ill|Ikutama Shrine|ja|生國魂神社|1=Ikukunitama Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Tennōji-ku, which is considered a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi" />
=== Tenjin Festival === The Tenjin Festival is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="Q426" />
It typically begins with the eve of the festival on July 24, followed by the land procession and boat procession on the 25th. The boat procession features a grand fleet of 100 boats magnificently navigating the Okawa River.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Sumiyoshi Festival === The Sumiyoshi Festival usually begins with the eve festival on July 30, followed by the main festival and the Nagoshi Harae (Summer Purification Ritual) on the 31st, and concludes with the Mikoshi Procession on August 1.<ref name="Q426" /> The Nagoshi Harae is famous for the ritual of passing through a ring of straw (chinowa-kuguri) and is designated as an intangible cultural asset of Osaka Prefecture.<ref name="Q426" /> The Mikoshi Procession involves carrying the portable shrine from Sumiyoshi Taisha to Shukuin Tongū in Sakai City.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Aizen Festival === The Aizen Festival is held annually from June 30 to July 2 and is also known as the "Yukata Festival."<ref name="Q426" />
=== Ikutama Festival === The Ikutama Festival of Ikutama Shrine (held annually from July 11 to 12) is sometimes included. While the Tenjin Festival is referred to as the "River Tenjin" due to its boat procession, the Ikutama Festival is called the "Land Ikutama" and features a land procession, making it a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/chuo/cmsfiles/contents/0000508/508145/nabi2ura.pdf Connecting People, Cities, and History Through Festivals: Shinno-san and Ikutama-san Festivals and Traditions] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>

'''Osaka's Three Great Summer Festivals''' (大阪三大夏祭り) are the three representative summer festivals held in [[Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[Osaka City|City]]: {{ill|Tenjin Festival|ja|天神祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Festival|ja|住吉祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Aizen Festival|ja|愛染まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizen Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="Q426">[https://www.osaka-city-callcenter.jp/faq/detail.aspx?id=426 Q. What are the summer festivals in Osaka City?] Osaka City Comprehensive Call Center (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> In some cases, it refers to the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and {{ill|Ikutama Festival|ja|生國魂祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutama Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="tennoji17">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000404/404034/tennoji17-07_p02.pdf Tennōji Ward Newsletter, No. 254, July 1, 2017] Tennōji Ward (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>

Due to their common nicknames—Aizen-san, Tenjin-san, and Sumiyoshi-san—there is a saying, "Osaka’s summer festivals begin with Aizen-san and end with Sumiyoshi-san." This saying is thought to be the origin of the phrase "ai (愛, love) sumi (住, live) masen (ません, sorry)," or as a mnemonic, "ai (愛) sumi (住) masen (天, Tenjin)," representing the three festivals.
In some cases, the term includes the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and the Ikutama Festival of {{ill|Ikutama Shrine|ja|生國魂神社|1=Ikukunitama Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutama Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikukunitama Shrine|qq=}} in Tennōji-ku, which is considered a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi" />
=== Tenjin Festival === The Tenjin Festival is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Japan|qq=}}.<ref name="Q426" />

=== Aizen Festival === The Aizen Festival is held annually from June 30 to July 2 and is also known as the "Yukata Festival."<ref name="Q426" />

While the Tenjin Festival is referred to as the "River Tenjin" due to its boat procession, the Ikutama Festival is called the "Land Ikutama" and features a land procession, making it a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/chuo/cmsfiles/contents/0000508/508145/nabi2ura.pdf Connecting People, Cities, and History Through Festivals: Shinno-san and Ikutama-san Festivals and Traditions] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>

Osaka--><!--Three Great Summer Festivals of Osaka-->

'''Osaka's Three Great Summer Festivals''' (大阪三大夏祭り) are the three representative summer festivals held in [[Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[Osaka City|City]]: {{ill|Tenjin Festival|ja|天神祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Festival|ja|住吉祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Aizen Festival|ja|愛染まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizen Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="Q426">[https://www.osaka-city-callcenter.jp/faq/detail.aspx?id=426 Q. What are the summer festivals in Osaka City?] Osaka City Comprehensive Call Center (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> In some cases, it refers to the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and {{ill|Ikutama Festival|ja|生國魂祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutama Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="tennoji17">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000404/404034/tennoji17-07_p02.pdf Tennōji Ward Newsletter, No. 254, July 1, 2017] Tennōji Ward (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>
== Explanation of Each Festival ==
The three festivals consist of the Tenjin Festival of {{ill|Osaka Tenmangu Shrine|ja|大阪天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Tenmangu Shrine|qq=}} in [[Kita-ku, Osaka|Kita-ku]], the Sumiyoshi Festival of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha|qq=}} in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], and the Aizen Festival at the {{ill|Shitennōji Temple|ja|四天王寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitennōji Temple|qq=}} branch Aizen-dō (Araryōzan Shōman'in) in [[Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennōji-ku]]. These are sometimes collectively referred to as Osaka’s Three Great Summer Festivals.<ref name="Q426" /><ref name="osaka2">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000241/241118/15041934_2.pdf JR Momodani Station] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> They are also called Naniwa’s Three Great Summer Festivals.
Due to their common nicknames—Aizen-san, Tenjin-san, and Sumiyoshi-san—there is a saying, "Osaka’s summer festivals begin with Aizen-san and end with Sumiyoshi-san." This saying is thought to be the origin of the phrase "ai (愛, love) sumi (住, live) masen (ません, sorry)," or as a mnemonic, "ai (愛) sumi (住) masen (天, Tenjin)," representing the three festivals.
In some cases, the term includes the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and the Ikutama Festival of {{ill|Ikutama Shrine|ja|生國魂神社|1=Ikukunitama Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutama Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikukunitama Shrine|qq=}} in Tennōji-ku, which is considered a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi" />
=== Tenjin Festival === The Tenjin Festival is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Japan|qq=}}.<ref name="Q426" />
It typically begins with the eve of the festival on July 24, followed by the land procession and boat procession on the 25th. The boat procession features a grand fleet of 100 boats magnificently navigating the Okawa River.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Sumiyoshi Festival === The Sumiyoshi Festival usually begins with the eve festival on July 30, followed by the main festival and the Nagoshi Harae (Summer Purification Ritual) on the 31st, and concludes with the Mikoshi Procession on August 1.<ref name="Q426" /> The Nagoshi Harae is famous for the ritual of passing through a ring of straw (chinowa-kuguri) and is designated as an intangible cultural asset of Osaka Prefecture.<ref name="Q426" /> The Mikoshi Procession involves carrying the portable shrine from Sumiyoshi Taisha to Shukuin Tongū in Sakai City.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Aizen Festival === The Aizen Festival is held annually from June 30 to July 2 and is also known as the "Yukata Festival."<ref name="Q426" />
=== Ikutama Festival === The Ikutama Festival of Ikutama Shrine (held annually from July 11 to 12) is sometimes included. While the Tenjin Festival is referred to as the "River Tenjin" due to its boat procession, the Ikutama Festival is called the "Land Ikutama" and features a land procession, making it a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/chuo/cmsfiles/contents/0000508/508145/nabi2ura.pdf Connecting People, Cities, and History Through Festivals: Shinno-san and Ikutama-san Festivals and Traditions] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>

'''Osaka's Three Great Summer Festivals''' (大阪三大夏祭り) are the three representative summer festivals held in [[Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[Osaka City|City]]: {{ill|Tenjin Festival|ja|天神祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Festival|ja|住吉祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Aizen Festival|ja|愛染まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizen Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="Q426">[https://www.osaka-city-callcenter.jp/faq/detail.aspx?id=426 Q. What are the summer festivals in Osaka City?] Osaka City Comprehensive Call Center (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> In some cases, it refers to the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and {{ill|Ikutama Festival|ja|生國魂祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutama Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="tennoji17">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000404/404034/tennoji17-07_p02.pdf Tennōji Ward Newsletter, No. 254, July 1, 2017] Tennōji Ward (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>
== Explanation of Each Festival ==
The three festivals consist of the Tenjin Festival of {{ill|Osaka Tenmangu Shrine|ja|大阪天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Tenmangu Shrine|qq=}} in [[Kita-ku, Osaka|Kita-ku]], the Sumiyoshi Festival of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha|qq=}} in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], and the Aizen Festival at the {{ill|Shitennōji Temple|ja|四天王寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitennōji Temple|qq=}} branch Aizen-dō (Araryōzan Shōman'in) in [[Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennōji-ku]]. These are sometimes collectively referred to as Osaka’s Three Great Summer Festivals.<ref name="Q426" /><ref name="osaka2">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000241/241118/15041934_2.pdf JR Momodani Station] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> They are also called Naniwa’s Three Great Summer Festivals.
Due to their common nicknames—Aizen-san, Tenjin-san, and Sumiyoshi-san—there is a saying, "Osaka’s summer festivals begin with Aizen-san and end with Sumiyoshi-san." This saying is thought to be the origin of the phrase "ai (愛, love) sumi (住, live) masen (ません, sorry)," or as a mnemonic, "ai (愛) sumi (住) masen (天, Tenjin)," representing the three festivals.
In some cases, the term includes the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and the Ikutama Festival of {{ill|Ikutama Shrine|ja|生國魂神社|1=Ikukunitama Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutama Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikukunitama Shrine|qq=}} in Tennōji-ku, which is considered a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi" />
=== Tenjin Festival === The Tenjin Festival is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Japan|qq=}}.<ref name="Q426" />
It typically begins with the eve of the festival on July 24, followed by the land procession and boat procession on the 25th. The boat procession features a grand fleet of 100 boats magnificently navigating the Okawa River.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Sumiyoshi Festival === The Sumiyoshi Festival usually begins with the eve festival on July 30, followed by the main festival and the Nagoshi Harae (Summer Purification Ritual) on the 31st, and concludes with the Mikoshi Procession on August 1.<ref name="Q426" /> The Nagoshi Harae is famous for the ritual of passing through a ring of straw (chinowa-kuguri) and is designated as an intangible cultural asset of Osaka Prefecture.<ref name="Q426" /> The Mikoshi Procession involves carrying the portable shrine from Sumiyoshi Taisha to Shukuin Tongū in Sakai City.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Aizen Festival === The Aizen Festival is held annually from June 30 to July 2 and is also known as the "Yukata Festival."<ref name="Q426" />
=== Ikutama Festival === The Ikutama Festival of Ikutama Shrine (held annually from July 11 to 12) is sometimes included. While the Tenjin Festival is referred to as the "River Tenjin" due to its boat procession, the Ikutama Festival is called the "Land Ikutama" and features a land procession, making it a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/chuo/cmsfiles/contents/0000508/508145/nabi2ura.pdf Connecting People, Cities, and History Through Festivals: Shinno-san and Ikutama-san Festivals and Traditions] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>

What are the summer festivals in Osaka City?] Osaka City Comprehensive Call Center (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> In some cases, it refers to the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and {{ill|Ikutama Festival|ja|生國魂祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutama Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="tennoji17">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000404/404034/tennoji17-07_p02.pdf Tennōji Ward Newsletter, No. 254, July 1, 2017] Tennōji Ward (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>

'''Osaka's Three Great Summer Festivals''' (大阪三大夏祭り) are the three representative summer festivals held in [[Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[Osaka City|City]]: {{ill|Tenjin Festival|ja|天神祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Festival|ja|住吉祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Aizen Festival|ja|愛染まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizen Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="Q426">[https://www.osaka-city-callcenter.jp/faq/detail.aspx?id=426 Q. What are the summer festivals in Osaka City?] Osaka City Comprehensive Call Center (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> In some cases, it refers to the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and {{ill|Ikutama Festival|ja|生國魂祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutama Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="tennoji17">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000404/404034/tennoji17-07_p02.pdf Tennōji Ward Newsletter, No. 254, July 1, 2017] Tennōji Ward (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>
== Explanation of Each Festival ==
The three festivals consist of the Tenjin Festival of {{ill|Osaka Tenmangu Shrine|ja|大阪天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Tenmangu Shrine|qq=}} in [[Kita-ku, Osaka|Kita-ku]], the Sumiyoshi Festival of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha|qq=}} in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], and the Aizen Festival at the {{ill|Shitennōji Temple|ja|四天王寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitennōji Temple|qq=}} branch Aizen-dō (Araryōzan Shōman'in) in [[Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennōji-ku]]. These are sometimes collectively referred to as Osaka’s Three Great Summer Festivals.<ref name="Q426" /><ref name="osaka2">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000241/241118/15041934_2.pdf JR Momodani Station] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> They are also called Naniwa’s Three Great Summer Festivals.
Due to their common nicknames—Aizen-san, Tenjin-san, and Sumiyoshi-san—there is a saying, "Osaka’s summer festivals begin with Aizen-san and end with Sumiyoshi-san." This saying is thought to be the origin of the phrase "ai (愛, love) sumi (住, live) masen (ません, sorry)," or as a mnemonic, "ai (愛) sumi (住) masen (天, Tenjin)," representing the three festivals.
In some cases, the term includes the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and the Ikutama Festival of {{ill|Ikutama Shrine|ja|生國魂神社|1=Ikukunitama Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutama Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikukunitama Shrine|qq=}} in Tennōji-ku, which is considered a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi" />
=== Tenjin Festival === The Tenjin Festival is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Japan|qq=}}.<ref name="Q426" />
It typically begins with the eve of the festival on July 24, followed by the land procession and boat procession on the 25th. The boat procession features a grand fleet of 100 boats magnificently navigating the Okawa River.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Sumiyoshi Festival === The Sumiyoshi Festival usually begins with the eve festival on July 30, followed by the main festival and the Nagoshi Harae (Summer Purification Ritual) on the 31st, and concludes with the Mikoshi Procession on August 1.<ref name="Q426" /> The Nagoshi Harae is famous for the ritual of passing through a ring of straw (chinowa-kuguri) and is designated as an intangible cultural asset of Osaka Prefecture.<ref name="Q426" /> The Mikoshi Procession involves carrying the portable shrine from Sumiyoshi Taisha to Shukuin Tongū in Sakai City.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Aizen Festival === The Aizen Festival is held annually from June 30 to July 2 and is also known as the "Yukata Festival."<ref name="Q426" />
=== Ikutama Festival === The Ikutama Festival of Ikutama Shrine (held annually from July 11 to 12) is sometimes included. While the Tenjin Festival is referred to as the "River Tenjin" due to its boat procession, the Ikutama Festival is called the "Land Ikutama" and features a land procession, making it a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/chuo/cmsfiles/contents/0000508/508145/nabi2ura.pdf Connecting People, Cities, and History Through Festivals: Shinno-san and Ikutama-san Festivals and Traditions] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>

'''Osaka's Three Great Summer Festivals''' (大阪三大夏祭り) are the three representative summer festivals held in [[Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[Osaka City|City]]: {{ill|Tenjin Festival|ja|天神祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Festival|ja|住吉祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Aizen Festival|ja|愛染まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizen Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="Q426">[https://www.osaka-city-callcenter.jp/faq/detail.aspx?id=426 Q. What are the summer festivals in Osaka City?] Osaka City Comprehensive Call Center (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> In some cases, it refers to the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and {{ill|Ikutama Festival|ja|生國魂祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutama Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="tennoji17">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000404/404034/tennoji17-07_p02.pdf Tennōji Ward Newsletter, No. 254, July 1, 2017] Tennōji Ward (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>
== Explanation of Each Festival ==
The three festivals consist of the Tenjin Festival of {{ill|Osaka Tenmangu Shrine|ja|大阪天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Tenmangu Shrine|qq=}} in [[Kita-ku, Osaka|Kita-ku]], the Sumiyoshi Festival of {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|lt=Sumiyoshi-taisha|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha|qq=}} in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], and the Aizen Festival at the {{ill|Shitennōji Temple|ja|四天王寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitennōji Temple|qq=}} branch Aizen-dō (Araryōzan Shōman'in) in [[Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennōji-ku]]. These are sometimes collectively referred to as Osaka’s Three Great Summer Festivals.<ref name="Q426" /><ref name="osaka2">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000241/241118/15041934_2.pdf JR Momodani Station] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> They are also called Naniwa’s Three Great Summer Festivals.
Due to their common nicknames—Aizen-san, Tenjin-san, and Sumiyoshi-san—there is a saying, "Osaka’s summer festivals begin with Aizen-san and end with Sumiyoshi-san." This saying is thought to be the origin of the phrase "ai (愛, love) sumi (住, live) masen (ません, sorry)," or as a mnemonic, "ai (愛) sumi (住) masen (天, Tenjin)," representing the three festivals.
In some cases, the term includes the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and the Ikutama Festival of {{ill|Ikutama Shrine|ja|生國魂神社|1=Ikukunitama Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutama Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikukunitama Shrine|qq=}} in Tennōji-ku, which is considered a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi" />
=== Tenjin Festival === The Tenjin Festival is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Japan|qq=}}.<ref name="Q426" />
It typically begins with the eve of the festival on July 24, followed by the land procession and boat procession on the 25th. The boat procession features a grand fleet of 100 boats magnificently navigating the Okawa River.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Sumiyoshi Festival === The Sumiyoshi Festival usually begins with the eve festival on July 30, followed by the main festival and the Nagoshi Harae (Summer Purification Ritual) on the 31st, and concludes with the Mikoshi Procession on August 1.<ref name="Q426" /> The Nagoshi Harae is famous for the ritual of passing through a ring of straw (chinowa-kuguri) and is designated as an intangible cultural asset of Osaka Prefecture.<ref name="Q426" /> The Mikoshi Procession involves carrying the portable shrine from Sumiyoshi Taisha to Shukuin Tongū in Sakai City.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Aizen Festival === The Aizen Festival is held annually from June 30 to July 2 and is also known as the "Yukata Festival."<ref name="Q426" />
=== Ikutama Festival === The Ikutama Festival of Ikutama Shrine (held annually from July 11 to 12) is sometimes included. While the Tenjin Festival is referred to as the "River Tenjin" due to its boat procession, the Ikutama Festival is called the "Land Ikutama" and features a land procession, making it a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/chuo/cmsfiles/contents/0000508/508145/nabi2ura.pdf Connecting People, Cities, and History Through Festivals: Shinno-san and Ikutama-san Festivals and Traditions] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>

The three festivals consist of the Tenjin Festival of [[:en:Osaka Tenmangū|Osaka Tenmangu Shrine]] in [[Kita-ku, Osaka|Kita-ku]], the Sumiyoshi Festival of [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], and the Aizen Festival at the [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitennōji Temple]] branch Aizen-dō (Araryōzan Shōman'in) in [[Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennōji-ku]]. These are sometimes collectively referred to as Osaka’s Three Great Summer Festivals.<ref name="Q426" /><ref name="osaka2">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000241/241118/15041934_2.pdf JR Momodani Station] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> They are also called Naniwa’s Three Great Summer Festivals.
Due to their common nicknames—Aizen-san, Tenjin-san, and Sumiyoshi-san—there is a saying, "Osaka’s summer festivals begin with Aizen-san and end with Sumiyoshi-san." This saying is thought to be the origin of the phrase "ai (愛, love) sumi (住, live) masen (ません, sorry)," or as a mnemonic, "ai (愛) sumi (住) masen (天, Tenjin)," representing the three festivals.
In some cases, the term includes the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and the Ikutama Festival of [[:en:Ikukunitama Shrine|Ikukunitama Shrine]] in Tennōji-ku, which is considered a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi" />
=== Tenjin Festival === The Tenjin Festival is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Japan|qq=}}.<ref name="Q426" />
It typically begins with the eve of the festival on July 24, followed by the land procession and boat procession on the 25th. The boat procession features a grand fleet of 100 boats magnificently navigating the Okawa River.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Sumiyoshi Festival === The Sumiyoshi Festival usually begins with the eve festival on July 30, followed by the main festival and the Nagoshi Harae (Summer Purification Ritual) on the 31st, and concludes with the Mikoshi Procession on August 1.<ref name="Q426" /> The Nagoshi Harae is famous for the ritual of passing through a ring of straw (chinowa-kuguri) and is designated as an intangible cultural asset of Osaka Prefecture.<ref name="Q426" /> The Mikoshi Procession involves carrying the portable shrine from Sumiyoshi Taisha to Shukuin Tongū in Sakai City.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Aizen Festival === The Aizen Festival is held annually from June 30 to July 2 and is also known as the "Yukata Festival."<ref name="Q426" />
=== Ikutama Festival === The Ikutama Festival of Ikutama Shrine (held annually from July 11 to 12) is sometimes included. While the Tenjin Festival is referred to as the "River Tenjin" due to its boat procession, the Ikutama Festival is called the "Land Ikutama" and features a land procession, making it a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/chuo/cmsfiles/contents/0000508/508145/nabi2ura.pdf Connecting People, Cities, and History Through Festivals: Shinno-san and Ikutama-san Festivals and Traditions] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>

'''Osaka's Three Great Summer Festivals''' (大阪三大夏祭り) are the three representative summer festivals held in [[Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[Osaka City|City]]: [[:en:Tenjin Matsuri|Tenjin Festival]], {{ill|Sumiyoshi Festival|ja|住吉祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Aizen Festival|ja|愛染まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizen Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="Q426">[https://www.osaka-city-callcenter.jp/faq/detail.aspx?id=426 Q. What are the summer festivals in Osaka City?] Osaka City Comprehensive Call Center (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> In some cases, it refers to the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and {{ill|Ikutama Festival|ja|生國魂祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutama Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="tennoji17">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000404/404034/tennoji17-07_p02.pdf Tennōji Ward Newsletter, No. 254, July 1, 2017] Tennōji Ward (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>
== Explanation of Each Festival ==
The three festivals consist of the Tenjin Festival of [[:en:Osaka Tenmangū|Osaka Tenmangu Shrine]] in [[Kita-ku, Osaka|Kita-ku]], the Sumiyoshi Festival of [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], and the Aizen Festival at the [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitennōji Temple]] branch Aizen-dō (Araryōzan Shōman'in) in [[Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennōji-ku]]. These are sometimes collectively referred to as Osaka’s Three Great Summer Festivals.<ref name="Q426" /><ref name="osaka2">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000241/241118/15041934_2.pdf JR Momodani Station] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> They are also called Naniwa’s Three Great Summer Festivals.
Due to their common nicknames—Aizen-san, Tenjin-san, and Sumiyoshi-san—there is a saying, "Osaka’s summer festivals begin with Aizen-san and end with Sumiyoshi-san." This saying is thought to be the origin of the phrase "ai (愛, love) sumi (住, live) masen (ません, sorry)," or as a mnemonic, "ai (愛) sumi (住) masen (天, Tenjin)," representing the three festivals.
In some cases, the term includes the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and the Ikutama Festival of [[:en:Ikukunitama Shrine|Ikukunitama Shrine]] in Tennōji-ku, which is considered a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi" />
=== Tenjin Festival === The Tenjin Festival is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Japan|qq=}}.<ref name="Q426" />
It typically begins with the eve of the festival on July 24, followed by the land procession and boat procession on the 25th. The boat procession features a grand fleet of 100 boats magnificently navigating the Okawa River.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Sumiyoshi Festival === The Sumiyoshi Festival usually begins with the eve festival on July 30, followed by the main festival and the Nagoshi Harae (Summer Purification Ritual) on the 31st, and concludes with the Mikoshi Procession on August 1.<ref name="Q426" /> The Nagoshi Harae is famous for the ritual of passing through a ring of straw (chinowa-kuguri) and is designated as an intangible cultural asset of Osaka Prefecture.<ref name="Q426" /> The Mikoshi Procession involves carrying the portable shrine from Sumiyoshi Taisha to Shukuin Tongū in Sakai City.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Aizen Festival === The Aizen Festival is held annually from June 30 to July 2 and is also known as the "Yukata Festival."<ref name="Q426" />
=== Ikutama Festival === The Ikutama Festival of Ikutama Shrine (held annually from July 11 to 12) is sometimes included. While the Tenjin Festival is referred to as the "River Tenjin" due to its boat procession, the Ikutama Festival is called the "Land Ikutama" and features a land procession, making it a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/chuo/cmsfiles/contents/0000508/508145/nabi2ura.pdf Connecting People, Cities, and History Through Festivals: Shinno-san and Ikutama-san Festivals and Traditions] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>
