'''Osaka's Three Great Summer Festivals''' (大阪三大夏祭り) are the three representative summer festivals held in [[Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[Osaka City|City]]: [[:en:Tenjin Matsuri|Tenjin Festival]], {{ill|Sumiyoshi Festival|ja|住吉祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Aizen Festival|ja|愛染まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizen Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="Q426">[https://www.osaka-city-callcenter.jp/faq/detail.aspx?id=426 Q. What are the summer festivals in Osaka City?] Osaka City Comprehensive Call Center (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> In some cases, it refers to the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and {{ill|Ikutama Festival|ja|生國魂祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutama Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="tennoji17">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000404/404034/tennoji17-07_p02.pdf Tennōji Ward Newsletter, No. 254, July 1, 2017] Tennōji Ward (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>

These are sometimes collectively referred to as Osaka’s Three Great Summer Festivals.<ref name="Q426" /><ref name="osaka2">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000241/241118/15041934_2.pdf JR Momodani Station] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> They are also called Naniwa’s Three Great Summer Festivals.
Due to their common nicknames—Aizen-san, Tenjin-san, and Sumiyoshi-san—there is a saying, "Osaka’s summer festivals begin with Aizen-san and end with Sumiyoshi-san." This saying is thought to be the origin of the phrase "ai (愛, love) sumi (住, live) masen (ません, sorry)," or as a mnemonic, "ai (愛) sumi (住) masen (天, Tenjin)," representing the three festivals.
In some cases, the term includes the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and the Ikutama Festival of [[:en:Ikukunitama Shrine|Ikukunitama Shrine]] in Tennōji-ku, which is considered a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi" />

It typically begins with the eve of the festival on July 24, followed by the land procession and boat procession on the 25th. The boat procession features a grand fleet of 100 boats magnificently navigating the Okawa River.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Sumiyoshi Festival === The Sumiyoshi Festival usually begins with the eve festival on July 30, followed by the main festival and the Nagoshi Harae (Summer Purification Ritual) on the 31st, and concludes with the Mikoshi Procession on August 1.<ref name="Q426" /> The Nagoshi Harae is famous for the ritual of passing through a ring of straw (chinowa-kuguri) and is designated as an intangible cultural asset of Osaka Prefecture.<ref name="Q426" /> The Mikoshi Procession involves carrying the portable shrine from Sumiyoshi Taisha to Shukuin Tongū in Sakai City.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Aizen Festival === The Aizen Festival is held annually from June 30 to July 2 and is also known as the "Yukata Festival."<ref name="Q426" />

While the Tenjin Festival is referred to as the "River Tenjin" due to its boat procession, the Ikutama Festival is called the "Land Ikutama" and features a land procession, making it a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/chuo/cmsfiles/contents/0000508/508145/nabi2ura.pdf Connecting People, Cities, and History Through Festivals: Shinno-san and Ikutama-san Festivals and Traditions] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>

'''Osaka's Three Great Summer Festivals''' (大阪三大夏祭り) are the three representative summer festivals held in [[Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[Osaka City|City]]: [[:en:Tenjin Matsuri|Tenjin Festival]], {{ill|Sumiyoshi Festival|ja|住吉祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Aizen Festival|ja|愛染まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizen Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="Q426">[https://www.osaka-city-callcenter.jp/faq/detail.aspx?id=426 Q. === Tenjin Festival === The Tenjin Festival is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Japan|qq=}}.<ref name="Q426" />
It typically begins with the eve of the festival on July 24, followed by the land procession and boat procession on the 25th. The boat procession features a grand fleet of 100 boats magnificently navigating the Okawa River.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Sumiyoshi Festival === The Sumiyoshi Festival usually begins with the eve festival on July 30, followed by the main festival and the Nagoshi Harae (Summer Purification Ritual) on the 31st, and concludes with the Mikoshi Procession on August 1.<ref name="Q426" /> The Nagoshi Harae is famous for the ritual of passing through a ring of straw (chinowa-kuguri) and is designated as an intangible cultural asset of Osaka Prefecture.<ref name="Q426" /> The Mikoshi Procession involves carrying the portable shrine from Sumiyoshi Taisha to Shukuin Tongū in Sakai City.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Aizen Festival === The Aizen Festival is held annually from June 30 to July 2 and is also known as the "Yukata Festival."<ref name="Q426" />
=== Ikutama Festival === The Ikutama Festival of Ikutama Shrine (held annually from July 11 to 12) is sometimes included. While the Tenjin Festival is referred to as the "River Tenjin" due to its boat procession, the Ikutama Festival is called the "Land Ikutama" and features a land procession, making it a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/chuo/cmsfiles/contents/0000508/508145/nabi2ura.pdf Connecting People, Cities, and History Through Festivals: Shinno-san and Ikutama-san Festivals and Traditions] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>

'''Osaka's Three Great Summer Festivals''' (大阪三大夏祭り) are the three representative summer festivals held in [[Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[Osaka City|City]]: [[:en:Tenjin Matsuri|Tenjin Festival]], {{ill|Sumiyoshi Festival|ja|住吉祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Aizen Festival|ja|愛染まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizen Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="Q426">[https://www.osaka-city-callcenter.jp/faq/detail.aspx?id=426 Q. What are the summer festivals in Osaka City?] Osaka City Comprehensive Call Center (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> In some cases, it refers to the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and {{ill|Ikutama Festival|ja|生國魂祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutama Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="tennoji17">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000404/404034/tennoji17-07_p02.pdf Tennōji Ward Newsletter, No. 254, July 1, 2017] Tennōji Ward (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>
== Explanation of Each Festival ==
The three festivals consist of the Tenjin Festival of [[:en:Osaka Tenmangū|Osaka Tenmangu Shrine]] in [[Kita-ku, Osaka|Kita-ku]], the Sumiyoshi Festival of [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], and the Aizen Festival at the [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitennōji Temple]] branch Aizen-dō (Araryōzan Shōman'in) in [[Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennōji-ku]]. These are sometimes collectively referred to as Osaka’s Three Great Summer Festivals.<ref name="Q426" /><ref name="osaka2">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000241/241118/15041934_2.pdf JR Momodani Station] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> They are also called Naniwa’s Three Great Summer Festivals.
Due to their common nicknames—Aizen-san, Tenjin-san, and Sumiyoshi-san—there is a saying, "Osaka’s summer festivals begin with Aizen-san and end with Sumiyoshi-san." This saying is thought to be the origin of the phrase "ai (愛, love) sumi (住, live) masen (ません, sorry)," or as a mnemonic, "ai (愛) sumi (住) masen (天, Tenjin)," representing the three festivals.
In some cases, the term includes the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and the Ikutama Festival of [[:en:Ikukunitama Shrine|Ikukunitama Shrine]] in Tennōji-ku, which is considered a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi" />
=== Tenjin Festival === The Tenjin Festival is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本三大一覧}}.<ref name="Q426" />
It typically begins with the eve of the festival on July 24, followed by the land procession and boat procession on the 25th. The boat procession features a grand fleet of 100 boats magnificently navigating the Okawa River.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Sumiyoshi Festival === The Sumiyoshi Festival usually begins with the eve festival on July 30, followed by the main festival and the Nagoshi Harae (Summer Purification Ritual) on the 31st, and concludes with the Mikoshi Procession on August 1.<ref name="Q426" /> The Nagoshi Harae is famous for the ritual of passing through a ring of straw (chinowa-kuguri) and is designated as an intangible cultural asset of Osaka Prefecture.<ref name="Q426" /> The Mikoshi Procession involves carrying the portable shrine from Sumiyoshi Taisha to Shukuin Tongū in Sakai City.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Aizen Festival === The Aizen Festival is held annually from June 30 to July 2 and is also known as the "Yukata Festival."<ref name="Q426" />
=== Ikutama Festival === The Ikutama Festival of Ikutama Shrine (held annually from July 11 to 12) is sometimes included. While the Tenjin Festival is referred to as the "River Tenjin" due to its boat procession, the Ikutama Festival is called the "Land Ikutama" and features a land procession, making it a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/chuo/cmsfiles/contents/0000508/508145/nabi2ura.pdf Connecting People, Cities, and History Through Festivals: Shinno-san and Ikutama-san Festivals and Traditions] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Japanese Culture
revision.text:
'''Osaka's Three Great Summer Festivals''' (大阪三大夏祭り) are the three representative summer festivals held in [[Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[Osaka City|City]]: [[:en:Tenjin Matsuri|Tenjin Festival]], {{ill|Sumiyoshi Festival|ja|住吉祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Aizen Festival|ja|愛染まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizen Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="Q426">[https://www.osaka-city-callcenter.jp/faq/detail.aspx?id=426 Q. What are the summer festivals in Osaka City?] Osaka City Comprehensive Call Center (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> In some cases, it refers to the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and {{ill|Ikutama Festival|ja|生國魂祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutama Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="tennoji17">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000404/404034/tennoji17-07_p02.pdf Tennōji Ward Newsletter, No. 254, July 1, 2017] Tennōji Ward (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>
== Explanation of Each Festival ==
The three festivals consist of the Tenjin Festival of [[:en:Osaka Tenmangū|Osaka Tenmangu Shrine]] in [[Kita-ku, Osaka|Kita-ku]], the Sumiyoshi Festival of [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], and the Aizen Festival at the [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitennōji Temple]] branch Aizen-dō (Araryōzan Shōman'in) in [[Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennōji-ku]]. These are sometimes collectively referred to as Osaka’s Three Great Summer Festivals.<ref name="Q426" /><ref name="osaka2">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000241/241118/15041934_2.pdf JR Momodani Station] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> They are also called Naniwa’s Three Great Summer Festivals.
Due to their common nicknames—Aizen-san, Tenjin-san, and Sumiyoshi-san—there is a saying, "Osaka’s summer festivals begin with Aizen-san and end with Sumiyoshi-san." This saying is thought to be the origin of the phrase "ai (愛, love) sumi (住, live) masen (ません, sorry)," or as a mnemonic, "ai (愛) sumi (住) masen (天, Tenjin)," representing the three festivals.
In some cases, the term includes the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and the Ikutama Festival of [[:en:Ikukunitama Shrine|Ikukunitama Shrine]] in Tennōji-ku, which is considered a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi" />
=== Tenjin Festival === The Tenjin Festival is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本三大一覧}}.<ref name="Q426" />
It typically begins with the eve of the festival on July 24, followed by the land procession and boat procession on the 25th. The boat procession features a grand fleet of 100 boats magnificently navigating the Okawa River.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Sumiyoshi Festival === The Sumiyoshi Festival usually begins with the eve festival on July 30, followed by the main festival and the Nagoshi Harae (Summer Purification Ritual) on the 31st, and concludes with the Mikoshi Procession on August 1.<ref name="Q426" /> The Nagoshi Harae is famous for the ritual of passing through a ring of straw (chinowa-kuguri) and is designated as an intangible cultural asset of Osaka Prefecture.<ref name="Q426" /> The Mikoshi Procession involves carrying the portable shrine from Sumiyoshi Taisha to Shukuin Tongū in Sakai City.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Aizen Festival === The Aizen Festival is held annually from June 30 to July 2 and is also known as the "Yukata Festival."<ref name="Q426" />
=== Ikutama Festival === The Ikutama Festival of Ikutama Shrine (held annually from July 11 to 12) is sometimes included. While the Tenjin Festival is referred to as the "River Tenjin" due to its boat procession, the Ikutama Festival is called the "Land Ikutama" and features a land procession, making it a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/chuo/cmsfiles/contents/0000508/508145/nabi2ura.pdf Connecting People, Cities, and History Through Festivals: Shinno-san and Ikutama-san Festivals and Traditions] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>

* {{ill|Haru Matsuri|lt=Haru Matsuri|lt=Spring Festivals|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Aki Matsuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Fuyu Matsuri|lt=Fuyu Matsuri|lt=Winter Festivals|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Winter Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Tanabata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabata|qq=}} * {{ill|Community building|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Community building|qq=}} * {{ill|Homecoming|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homecoming|qq=}} * {{ill|Summer Vacation|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Vacation|qq=}}

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Summer festival
revision.text:
'''Osaka's Three Great Summer Festivals''' (大阪三大夏祭り) are the three representative summer festivals held in [[Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[Osaka City|City]]: [[:en:Tenjin Matsuri|Tenjin Festival]], {{ill|Sumiyoshi Festival|ja|住吉祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Aizen Festival|ja|愛染まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizen Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="Q426">[https://www.osaka-city-callcenter.jp/faq/detail.aspx?id=426 Q. What are the summer festivals in Osaka City?] Osaka City Comprehensive Call Center (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> In some cases, it refers to the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and {{ill|Ikutama Festival|ja|生國魂祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutama Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="tennoji17">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000404/404034/tennoji17-07_p02.pdf Tennōji Ward Newsletter, No. 254, July 1, 2017] Tennōji Ward (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>
== Explanation of Each Festival ==
The three festivals consist of the Tenjin Festival of [[:en:Osaka Tenmangū|Osaka Tenmangu Shrine]] in [[Kita-ku, Osaka|Kita-ku]], the Sumiyoshi Festival of [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], and the Aizen Festival at the [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitennōji Temple]] branch Aizen-dō (Araryōzan Shōman'in) in [[Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennōji-ku]]. These are sometimes collectively referred to as Osaka’s Three Great Summer Festivals.<ref name="Q426" /><ref name="osaka2">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000241/241118/15041934_2.pdf JR Momodani Station] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> They are also called Naniwa’s Three Great Summer Festivals.
Due to their common nicknames—Aizen-san, Tenjin-san, and Sumiyoshi-san—there is a saying, "Osaka’s summer festivals begin with Aizen-san and end with Sumiyoshi-san." This saying is thought to be the origin of the phrase "ai (愛, love) sumi (住, live) masen (ません, sorry)," or as a mnemonic, "ai (愛) sumi (住) masen (天, Tenjin)," representing the three festivals.
In some cases, the term includes the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and the Ikutama Festival of [[:en:Ikukunitama Shrine|Ikukunitama Shrine]] in Tennōji-ku, which is considered a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi" />
=== Tenjin Festival === The Tenjin Festival is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本三大一覧}}.<ref name="Q426" />
It typically begins with the eve of the festival on July 24, followed by the land procession and boat procession on the 25th. The boat procession features a grand fleet of 100 boats magnificently navigating the Okawa River.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Sumiyoshi Festival === The Sumiyoshi Festival usually begins with the eve festival on July 30, followed by the main festival and the Nagoshi Harae (Summer Purification Ritual) on the 31st, and concludes with the Mikoshi Procession on August 1.<ref name="Q426" /> The Nagoshi Harae is famous for the ritual of passing through a ring of straw (chinowa-kuguri) and is designated as an intangible cultural asset of Osaka Prefecture.<ref name="Q426" /> The Mikoshi Procession involves carrying the portable shrine from Sumiyoshi Taisha to Shukuin Tongū in Sakai City.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Aizen Festival === The Aizen Festival is held annually from June 30 to July 2 and is also known as the "Yukata Festival."<ref name="Q426" />
=== Ikutama Festival === The Ikutama Festival of Ikutama Shrine (held annually from July 11 to 12) is sometimes included. While the Tenjin Festival is referred to as the "River Tenjin" due to its boat procession, the Ikutama Festival is called the "Land Ikutama" and features a land procession, making it a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/chuo/cmsfiles/contents/0000508/508145/nabi2ura.pdf Connecting People, Cities, and History Through Festivals: Shinno-san and Ikutama-san Festivals and Traditions] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>

* {{ill|Haru Matsuri|lt=Haru Matsuri|lt=Spring Festivals|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Aki Matsuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Fuyu Matsuri|lt=Fuyu Matsuri|lt=Winter Festivals|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Winter Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Tanabata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabata|qq=}} * {{ill|Community building|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Community building|qq=}} * {{ill|Homecoming|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homecoming|qq=}} * {{ill|Summer Vacation|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Vacation|qq=}}

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Three Great Summer Festivals of Osaka
revision.text:
'''Osaka's Three Great Summer Festivals''' (大阪三大夏祭り) are the three representative summer festivals held in [[Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[Osaka City|City]]: [[:en:Tenjin Matsuri|Tenjin Festival]], {{ill|Sumiyoshi Festival|ja|住吉祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Aizen Festival|ja|愛染まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizen Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="Q426">[https://www.osaka-city-callcenter.jp/faq/detail.aspx?id=426 Q. What are the summer festivals in Osaka City?] Osaka City Comprehensive Call Center (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> In some cases, it refers to the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and {{ill|Ikutama Festival|ja|生國魂祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutama Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="tennoji17">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000404/404034/tennoji17-07_p02.pdf Tennōji Ward Newsletter, No. 254, July 1, 2017] Tennōji Ward (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>
== Explanation of Each Festival ==
The three festivals consist of the Tenjin Festival of [[:en:Osaka Tenmangū|Osaka Tenmangu Shrine]] in [[Kita-ku, Osaka|Kita-ku]], the Sumiyoshi Festival of [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], and the Aizen Festival at the [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitennōji Temple]] branch Aizen-dō (Araryōzan Shōman'in) in [[Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennōji-ku]]. These are sometimes collectively referred to as Osaka’s Three Great Summer Festivals.<ref name="Q426" /><ref name="osaka2">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000241/241118/15041934_2.pdf JR Momodani Station] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> They are also called Naniwa’s Three Great Summer Festivals.
Due to their common nicknames—Aizen-san, Tenjin-san, and Sumiyoshi-san—there is a saying, "Osaka’s summer festivals begin with Aizen-san and end with Sumiyoshi-san." This saying is thought to be the origin of the phrase "ai (愛, love) sumi (住, live) masen (ません, sorry)," or as a mnemonic, "ai (愛) sumi (住) masen (天, Tenjin)," representing the three festivals.
In some cases, the term includes the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and the Ikutama Festival of [[:en:Ikukunitama Shrine|Ikukunitama Shrine]] in Tennōji-ku, which is considered a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi" />
=== Tenjin Festival === The Tenjin Festival is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本三大一覧}}.<ref name="Q426" />
It typically begins with the eve of the festival on July 24, followed by the land procession and boat procession on the 25th. The boat procession features a grand fleet of 100 boats magnificently navigating the Okawa River.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Sumiyoshi Festival === The Sumiyoshi Festival usually begins with the eve festival on July 30, followed by the main festival and the Nagoshi Harae (Summer Purification Ritual) on the 31st, and concludes with the Mikoshi Procession on August 1.<ref name="Q426" /> The Nagoshi Harae is famous for the ritual of passing through a ring of straw (chinowa-kuguri) and is designated as an intangible cultural asset of Osaka Prefecture.<ref name="Q426" /> The Mikoshi Procession involves carrying the portable shrine from Sumiyoshi Taisha to Shukuin Tongū in Sakai City.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Aizen Festival === The Aizen Festival is held annually from June 30 to July 2 and is also known as the "Yukata Festival."<ref name="Q426" />
=== Ikutama Festival === The Ikutama Festival of Ikutama Shrine (held annually from July 11 to 12) is sometimes included. While the Tenjin Festival is referred to as the "River Tenjin" due to its boat procession, the Ikutama Festival is called the "Land Ikutama" and features a land procession, making it a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/chuo/cmsfiles/contents/0000508/508145/nabi2ura.pdf Connecting People, Cities, and History Through Festivals: Shinno-san and Ikutama-san Festivals and Traditions] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>

* {{ill|Haru Matsuri|lt=Haru Matsuri|lt=Spring Festivals|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Aki Matsuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Fuyu Matsuri|lt=Fuyu Matsuri|lt=Winter Festivals|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Winter Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Tanabata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabata|qq=}} * {{ill|Community building|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Community building|qq=}} * {{ill|Homecoming|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homecoming|qq=}} * {{ill|Summer Vacation|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Vacation|qq=}}

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:三大祭り]] into [[Category:Three Major Festivals]] via bot
revision.text:
'''Osaka's Three Great Summer Festivals''' (大阪三大夏祭り) are the three representative summer festivals held in [[Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[Osaka City|City]]: [[:en:Tenjin Matsuri|Tenjin Festival]], {{ill|Sumiyoshi Festival|ja|住吉祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Aizen Festival|ja|愛染まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizen Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="Q426">[https://www.osaka-city-callcenter.jp/faq/detail.aspx?id=426 Q. What are the summer festivals in Osaka City?] Osaka City Comprehensive Call Center (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> In some cases, it refers to the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and {{ill|Ikutama Festival|ja|生國魂祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutama Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="tennoji17">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000404/404034/tennoji17-07_p02.pdf Tennōji Ward Newsletter, No. 254, July 1, 2017] Tennōji Ward (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>
== Explanation of Each Festival ==
The three festivals consist of the Tenjin Festival of [[:en:Osaka Tenmangū|Osaka Tenmangu Shrine]] in [[:en:Kita-ku, Osaka|Kita-ku]], the Sumiyoshi Festival of [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], and the Aizen Festival at the [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitennōji Temple]] branch Aizen-dō (Araryōzan Shōman'in) in [[Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennōji-ku]]. These are sometimes collectively referred to as Osaka’s Three Great Summer Festivals.<ref name="Q426" /><ref name="osaka2">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000241/241118/15041934_2.pdf JR Momodani Station] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> They are also called Naniwa’s Three Great Summer Festivals.
Due to their common nicknames—Aizen-san, Tenjin-san, and Sumiyoshi-san—there is a saying, "Osaka’s summer festivals begin with Aizen-san and end with Sumiyoshi-san." This saying is thought to be the origin of the phrase "ai (愛, love) sumi (住, live) masen (ません, sorry)," or as a mnemonic, "ai (愛) sumi (住) masen (天, Tenjin)," representing the three festivals.
In some cases, the term includes the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and the Ikutama Festival of [[:en:Ikukunitama Shrine|Ikukunitama Shrine]] in Tennōji-ku, which is considered a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi" />
=== Tenjin Festival === The Tenjin Festival is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本三大一覧}}.<ref name="Q426" />
It typically begins with the eve of the festival on July 24, followed by the land procession and boat procession on the 25th. The boat procession features a grand fleet of 100 boats magnificently navigating the Okawa River.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Sumiyoshi Festival === The Sumiyoshi Festival usually begins with the eve festival on July 30, followed by the main festival and the Nagoshi Harae (Summer Purification Ritual) on the 31st, and concludes with the Mikoshi Procession on August 1.<ref name="Q426" /> The Nagoshi Harae is famous for the ritual of passing through a ring of straw (chinowa-kuguri) and is designated as an intangible cultural asset of Osaka Prefecture.<ref name="Q426" /> The Mikoshi Procession involves carrying the portable shrine from Sumiyoshi Taisha to Shukuin Tongū in Sakai City.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Aizen Festival === The Aizen Festival is held annually from June 30 to July 2 and is also known as the "Yukata Festival."<ref name="Q426" />
=== Ikutama Festival === The Ikutama Festival of Ikutama Shrine (held annually from July 11 to 12) is sometimes included. While the Tenjin Festival is referred to as the "River Tenjin" due to its boat procession, the Ikutama Festival is called the "Land Ikutama" and features a land procession, making it a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/chuo/cmsfiles/contents/0000508/508145/nabi2ura.pdf Connecting People, Cities, and History Through Festivals: Shinno-san and Ikutama-san Festivals and Traditions] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>

* {{ill|Haru Matsuri|lt=Haru Matsuri|lt=Spring Festivals|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Aki Matsuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Fuyu Matsuri|lt=Fuyu Matsuri|lt=Winter Festivals|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Winter Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Tanabata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabata|qq=}} * {{ill|Community building|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Community building|qq=}} * {{ill|Homecoming|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homecoming|qq=}} * {{ill|Summer Vacation|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Vacation|qq=}}

revision.text:
'''Osaka's Three Great Summer Festivals''' (大阪三大夏祭り) are the three representative summer festivals held in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[:en:Osaka City|City]]: [[:en:Tenjin Matsuri|Tenjin Festival]], {{ill|Sumiyoshi Festival|ja|住吉祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Aizen Festival|ja|愛染まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizen Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="Q426">[https://www.osaka-city-callcenter.jp/faq/detail.aspx?id=426 Q. What are the summer festivals in Osaka City?] Osaka City Comprehensive Call Center (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> In some cases, it refers to the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and {{ill|Ikutama Festival|ja|生國魂祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutama Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="tennoji17">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000404/404034/tennoji17-07_p02.pdf Tennōji Ward Newsletter, No. 254, July 1, 2017] Tennōji Ward (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>
== Explanation of Each Festival ==
The three festivals consist of the Tenjin Festival of [[:en:Osaka Tenmangū|Osaka Tenmangu Shrine]] in [[:en:Kita-ku, Osaka|Kita-ku]], the Sumiyoshi Festival of [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], and the Aizen Festival at the [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitennōji Temple]] branch Aizen-dō (Araryōzan Shōman'in) in [[Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennōji-ku]]. These are sometimes collectively referred to as Osaka’s Three Great Summer Festivals.<ref name="Q426" /><ref name="osaka2">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000241/241118/15041934_2.pdf JR Momodani Station] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> They are also called Naniwa’s Three Great Summer Festivals.
Due to their common nicknames—Aizen-san, Tenjin-san, and Sumiyoshi-san—there is a saying, "Osaka’s summer festivals begin with Aizen-san and end with Sumiyoshi-san." This saying is thought to be the origin of the phrase "ai (愛, love) sumi (住, live) masen (ません, sorry)," or as a mnemonic, "ai (愛) sumi (住) masen (天, Tenjin)," representing the three festivals.
In some cases, the term includes the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and the Ikutama Festival of [[:en:Ikukunitama Shrine|Ikukunitama Shrine]] in Tennōji-ku, which is considered a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi" />
=== Tenjin Festival === The Tenjin Festival is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本三大一覧}}.<ref name="Q426" />
It typically begins with the eve of the festival on July 24, followed by the land procession and boat procession on the 25th. The boat procession features a grand fleet of 100 boats magnificently navigating the Okawa River.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Sumiyoshi Festival === The Sumiyoshi Festival usually begins with the eve festival on July 30, followed by the main festival and the Nagoshi Harae (Summer Purification Ritual) on the 31st, and concludes with the Mikoshi Procession on August 1.<ref name="Q426" /> The Nagoshi Harae is famous for the ritual of passing through a ring of straw (chinowa-kuguri) and is designated as an intangible cultural asset of Osaka Prefecture.<ref name="Q426" /> The Mikoshi Procession involves carrying the portable shrine from Sumiyoshi Taisha to Shukuin Tongū in Sakai City.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Aizen Festival === The Aizen Festival is held annually from June 30 to July 2 and is also known as the "Yukata Festival."<ref name="Q426" />
=== Ikutama Festival === The Ikutama Festival of Ikutama Shrine (held annually from July 11 to 12) is sometimes included. While the Tenjin Festival is referred to as the "River Tenjin" due to its boat procession, the Ikutama Festival is called the "Land Ikutama" and features a land procession, making it a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/chuo/cmsfiles/contents/0000508/508145/nabi2ura.pdf Connecting People, Cities, and History Through Festivals: Shinno-san and Ikutama-san Festivals and Traditions] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>

* {{ill|Haru Matsuri|lt=Haru Matsuri|lt=Spring Festivals|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Aki Matsuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Fuyu Matsuri|lt=Fuyu Matsuri|lt=Winter Festivals|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Winter Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Tanabata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabata|qq=}} * {{ill|Community building|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Community building|qq=}} * {{ill|Homecoming|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homecoming|qq=}} * {{ill|Summer Vacation|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Vacation|qq=}}

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:大阪市の祭り]] into [[Category:Festivals of Osaka City]] via bot
revision.text:
'''Osaka's Three Great Summer Festivals''' (大阪三大夏祭り) are the three representative summer festivals held in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[:en:Osaka City|City]]: [[:en:Tenjin Matsuri|Tenjin Festival]], {{ill|Sumiyoshi Festival|ja|住吉祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Aizen Festival|ja|愛染まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizen Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="Q426">[https://www.osaka-city-callcenter.jp/faq/detail.aspx?id=426 Q. What are the summer festivals in Osaka City?] Osaka City Comprehensive Call Center (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> In some cases, it refers to the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and {{ill|Ikutama Festival|ja|生國魂祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutama Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="tennoji17">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000404/404034/tennoji17-07_p02.pdf Tennōji Ward Newsletter, No. 254, July 1, 2017] Tennōji Ward (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>
== Explanation of Each Festival ==
The three festivals consist of the Tenjin Festival of [[:en:Osaka Tenmangū|Osaka Tenmangu Shrine]] in [[:en:Kita-ku, Osaka|Kita-ku]], the Sumiyoshi Festival of [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], and the Aizen Festival at the [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitennōji Temple]] branch Aizen-dō (Araryōzan Shōman'in) in [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennōji-ku]]. These are sometimes collectively referred to as Osaka’s Three Great Summer Festivals.<ref name="Q426" /><ref name="osaka2">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000241/241118/15041934_2.pdf JR Momodani Station] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> They are also called Naniwa’s Three Great Summer Festivals.
Due to their common nicknames—Aizen-san, Tenjin-san, and Sumiyoshi-san—there is a saying, "Osaka’s summer festivals begin with Aizen-san and end with Sumiyoshi-san." This saying is thought to be the origin of the phrase "ai (愛, love) sumi (住, live) masen (ません, sorry)," or as a mnemonic, "ai (愛) sumi (住) masen (天, Tenjin)," representing the three festivals.
In some cases, the term includes the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and the Ikutama Festival of [[:en:Ikukunitama Shrine|Ikukunitama Shrine]] in Tennōji-ku, which is considered a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi" />
=== Tenjin Festival === The Tenjin Festival is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本三大一覧}}.<ref name="Q426" />
It typically begins with the eve of the festival on July 24, followed by the land procession and boat procession on the 25th. The boat procession features a grand fleet of 100 boats magnificently navigating the Okawa River.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Sumiyoshi Festival === The Sumiyoshi Festival usually begins with the eve festival on July 30, followed by the main festival and the Nagoshi Harae (Summer Purification Ritual) on the 31st, and concludes with the Mikoshi Procession on August 1.<ref name="Q426" /> The Nagoshi Harae is famous for the ritual of passing through a ring of straw (chinowa-kuguri) and is designated as an intangible cultural asset of Osaka Prefecture.<ref name="Q426" /> The Mikoshi Procession involves carrying the portable shrine from Sumiyoshi Taisha to Shukuin Tongū in Sakai City.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Aizen Festival === The Aizen Festival is held annually from June 30 to July 2 and is also known as the "Yukata Festival."<ref name="Q426" />
=== Ikutama Festival === The Ikutama Festival of Ikutama Shrine (held annually from July 11 to 12) is sometimes included. While the Tenjin Festival is referred to as the "River Tenjin" due to its boat procession, the Ikutama Festival is called the "Land Ikutama" and features a land procession, making it a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/chuo/cmsfiles/contents/0000508/508145/nabi2ura.pdf Connecting People, Cities, and History Through Festivals: Shinno-san and Ikutama-san Festivals and Traditions] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>

* {{ill|Haru Matsuri|lt=Haru Matsuri|lt=Spring Festivals|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Aki Matsuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Fuyu Matsuri|lt=Fuyu Matsuri|lt=Winter Festivals|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Winter Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Tanabata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabata|qq=}} * {{ill|Community building|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Community building|qq=}} * {{ill|Homecoming|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homecoming|qq=}} * {{ill|Summer Vacation|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Vacation|qq=}}

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:Festivals of Japan]] into [[Category:Festivals in Japan]] via bot
revision.text:
'''Osaka's Three Great Summer Festivals''' (大阪三大夏祭り) are the three representative summer festivals held in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[:en:Osaka City|City]]: [[:en:Tenjin Matsuri|Tenjin Festival]], {{ill|Sumiyoshi Festival|ja|住吉祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Aizen Festival|ja|愛染まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizen Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="Q426">[https://www.osaka-city-callcenter.jp/faq/detail.aspx?id=426 Q. What are the summer festivals in Osaka City?] Osaka City Comprehensive Call Center (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> In some cases, it refers to the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and {{ill|Ikutama Festival|ja|生國魂祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutama Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="tennoji17">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000404/404034/tennoji17-07_p02.pdf Tennōji Ward Newsletter, No. 254, July 1, 2017] Tennōji Ward (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>
== Explanation of Each Festival ==
The three festivals consist of the Tenjin Festival of [[:en:Osaka Tenmangū|Osaka Tenmangu Shrine]] in [[:en:Kita-ku, Osaka|Kita-ku]], the Sumiyoshi Festival of [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], and the Aizen Festival at the [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitennōji Temple]] branch Aizen-dō (Araryōzan Shōman'in) in [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennōji-ku]]. These are sometimes collectively referred to as Osaka’s Three Great Summer Festivals.<ref name="Q426" /><ref name="osaka2">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000241/241118/15041934_2.pdf JR Momodani Station] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> They are also called Naniwa’s Three Great Summer Festivals.
Due to their common nicknames—Aizen-san, Tenjin-san, and Sumiyoshi-san—there is a saying, "Osaka’s summer festivals begin with Aizen-san and end with Sumiyoshi-san." This saying is thought to be the origin of the phrase "ai (愛, love) sumi (住, live) masen (ません, sorry)," or as a mnemonic, "ai (愛) sumi (住) masen (天, Tenjin)," representing the three festivals.
In some cases, the term includes the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and the Ikutama Festival of [[:en:Ikukunitama Shrine|Ikukunitama Shrine]] in Tennōji-ku, which is considered a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi" />
=== Tenjin Festival === The Tenjin Festival is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本三大一覧}}.<ref name="Q426" />
It typically begins with the eve of the festival on July 24, followed by the land procession and boat procession on the 25th. The boat procession features a grand fleet of 100 boats magnificently navigating the Okawa River.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Sumiyoshi Festival === The Sumiyoshi Festival usually begins with the eve festival on July 30, followed by the main festival and the Nagoshi Harae (Summer Purification Ritual) on the 31st, and concludes with the Mikoshi Procession on August 1.<ref name="Q426" /> The Nagoshi Harae is famous for the ritual of passing through a ring of straw (chinowa-kuguri) and is designated as an intangible cultural asset of Osaka Prefecture.<ref name="Q426" /> The Mikoshi Procession involves carrying the portable shrine from Sumiyoshi Taisha to Shukuin Tongū in Sakai City.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Aizen Festival === The Aizen Festival is held annually from June 30 to July 2 and is also known as the "Yukata Festival."<ref name="Q426" />
=== Ikutama Festival === The Ikutama Festival of Ikutama Shrine (held annually from July 11 to 12) is sometimes included. While the Tenjin Festival is referred to as the "River Tenjin" due to its boat procession, the Ikutama Festival is called the "Land Ikutama" and features a land procession, making it a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/chuo/cmsfiles/contents/0000508/508145/nabi2ura.pdf Connecting People, Cities, and History Through Festivals: Shinno-san and Ikutama-san Festivals and Traditions] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>

* {{ill|Haru Matsuri|lt=Haru Matsuri|lt=Spring Festivals|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Aki Matsuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Fuyu Matsuri|lt=Fuyu Matsuri|lt=Winter Festivals|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Winter Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Tanabata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabata|qq=}} * {{ill|Community building|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Community building|qq=}} * {{ill|Homecoming|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homecoming|qq=}} * {{ill|Summer Vacation|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Vacation|qq=}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11440018]] Wikidata name: Three Great Summer Festivals of Osaka [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Osaka's Three Great Summer Festivals''' (大阪三大夏祭り) are the three representative summer festivals held in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[:en:Osaka City|City]]: [[:en:Tenjin Matsuri|Tenjin Festival]], {{ill|Sumiyoshi Festival|ja|住吉祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Aizen Festival|ja|愛染まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizen Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="Q426">[https://www.osaka-city-callcenter.jp/faq/detail.aspx?id=426 Q. What are the summer festivals in Osaka City?] Osaka City Comprehensive Call Center (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> In some cases, it refers to the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and {{ill|Ikutama Festival|ja|生國魂祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutama Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="tennoji17">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000404/404034/tennoji17-07_p02.pdf Tennōji Ward Newsletter, No. 254, July 1, 2017] Tennōji Ward (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>
== Explanation of Each Festival ==
The three festivals consist of the Tenjin Festival of [[:en:Osaka Tenmangū|Osaka Tenmangu Shrine]] in [[:en:Kita-ku, Osaka|Kita-ku]], the Sumiyoshi Festival of [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], and the Aizen Festival at the [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitennōji Temple]] branch Aizen-dō (Araryōzan Shōman'in) in [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennōji-ku]]. These are sometimes collectively referred to as Osaka’s Three Great Summer Festivals.<ref name="Q426" /><ref name="osaka2">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000241/241118/15041934_2.pdf JR Momodani Station] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> They are also called Naniwa’s Three Great Summer Festivals.
Due to their common nicknames—Aizen-san, Tenjin-san, and Sumiyoshi-san—there is a saying, "Osaka’s summer festivals begin with Aizen-san and end with Sumiyoshi-san." This saying is thought to be the origin of the phrase "ai (愛, love) sumi (住, live) masen (ません, sorry)," or as a mnemonic, "ai (愛) sumi (住) masen (天, Tenjin)," representing the three festivals.
In some cases, the term includes the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and the Ikutama Festival of [[:en:Ikukunitama Shrine|Ikukunitama Shrine]] in Tennōji-ku, which is considered a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi" />
=== Tenjin Festival === The Tenjin Festival is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本三大一覧}}.<ref name="Q426" />
It typically begins with the eve of the festival on July 24, followed by the land procession and boat procession on the 25th. The boat procession features a grand fleet of 100 boats magnificently navigating the Okawa River.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Sumiyoshi Festival === The Sumiyoshi Festival usually begins with the eve festival on July 30, followed by the main festival and the Nagoshi Harae (Summer Purification Ritual) on the 31st, and concludes with the Mikoshi Procession on August 1.<ref name="Q426" /> The Nagoshi Harae is famous for the ritual of passing through a ring of straw (chinowa-kuguri) and is designated as an intangible cultural asset of Osaka Prefecture.<ref name="Q426" /> The Mikoshi Procession involves carrying the portable shrine from Sumiyoshi Taisha to Shukuin Tongū in Sakai City.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Aizen Festival === The Aizen Festival is held annually from June 30 to July 2 and is also known as the "Yukata Festival."<ref name="Q426" />
=== Ikutama Festival === The Ikutama Festival of Ikutama Shrine (held annually from July 11 to 12) is sometimes included. While the Tenjin Festival is referred to as the "River Tenjin" due to its boat procession, the Ikutama Festival is called the "Land Ikutama" and features a land procession, making it a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/chuo/cmsfiles/contents/0000508/508145/nabi2ura.pdf Connecting People, Cities, and History Through Festivals: Shinno-san and Ikutama-san Festivals and Traditions] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11440018]] Wikidata name: Three Great Summer Festivals of Osaka [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Osaka's Three Great Summer Festivals''' (大阪三大夏祭り) are the three representative summer festivals held in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[:en:Osaka City|City]]: [[:en:Tenjin Matsuri|Tenjin Festival]], {{ill|Sumiyoshi Festival|ja|住吉祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Aizen Festival|ja|愛染まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizen Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="Q426">[https://www.osaka-city-callcenter.jp/faq/detail.aspx?id=426 Q. What are the summer festivals in Osaka City?] Osaka City Comprehensive Call Center (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> In some cases, it refers to the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and {{ill|Ikutama Festival|ja|生國魂祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutama Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="tennoji17">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000404/404034/tennoji17-07_p02.pdf Tennōji Ward Newsletter, No. 254, July 1, 2017] Tennōji Ward (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>
== Explanation of Each Festival ==
The three festivals consist of the Tenjin Festival of [[:en:Osaka Tenmangū|Osaka Tenmangu Shrine]] in [[:en:Kita-ku, Osaka|Kita-ku]], the Sumiyoshi Festival of [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], and the Aizen Festival at the [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitennōji Temple]] branch Aizen-dō (Araryōzan Shōman'in) in [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennōji-ku]]. These are sometimes collectively referred to as Osaka’s Three Great Summer Festivals.<ref name="Q426" /><ref name="osaka2">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000241/241118/15041934_2.pdf JR Momodani Station] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> They are also called Naniwa’s Three Great Summer Festivals.
Due to their common nicknames—Aizen-san, Tenjin-san, and Sumiyoshi-san—there is a saying, "Osaka’s summer festivals begin with Aizen-san and end with Sumiyoshi-san." This saying is thought to be the origin of the phrase "ai (愛, love) sumi (住, live) masen (ません, sorry)," or as a mnemonic, "ai (愛) sumi (住) masen (天, Tenjin)," representing the three festivals.
In some cases, the term includes the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and the Ikutama Festival of [[:en:Ikukunitama Shrine|Ikukunitama Shrine]] in Tennōji-ku, which is considered a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi" />
=== Tenjin Festival === The Tenjin Festival is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本三大一覧|1=Japan's Top 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10311536}}.<ref name="Q426" />
It typically begins with the eve of the festival on July 24, followed by the land procession and boat procession on the 25th. The boat procession features a grand fleet of 100 boats magnificently navigating the Okawa River.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Sumiyoshi Festival === The Sumiyoshi Festival usually begins with the eve festival on July 30, followed by the main festival and the Nagoshi Harae (Summer Purification Ritual) on the 31st, and concludes with the Mikoshi Procession on August 1.<ref name="Q426" /> The Nagoshi Harae is famous for the ritual of passing through a ring of straw (chinowa-kuguri) and is designated as an intangible cultural asset of Osaka Prefecture.<ref name="Q426" /> The Mikoshi Procession involves carrying the portable shrine from Sumiyoshi Taisha to Shukuin Tongū in Sakai City.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Aizen Festival === The Aizen Festival is held annually from June 30 to July 2 and is also known as the "Yukata Festival."<ref name="Q426" />
=== Ikutama Festival === The Ikutama Festival of Ikutama Shrine (held annually from July 11 to 12) is sometimes included. While the Tenjin Festival is referred to as the "River Tenjin" due to its boat procession, the Ikutama Festival is called the "Land Ikutama" and features a land procession, making it a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/chuo/cmsfiles/contents/0000508/508145/nabi2ura.pdf Connecting People, Cities, and History Through Festivals: Shinno-san and Ikutama-san Festivals and Traditions] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11440018]] Wikidata name: Three Great Summer Festivals of Osaka [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Osaka's Three Great Summer Festivals''' (大阪三大夏祭り) are the three representative summer festivals held in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[:en:Osaka City|City]]: [[:en:Tenjin Matsuri|Tenjin Festival]], {{ill|Sumiyoshi Festival|ja|住吉祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Aizen Festival|ja|愛染まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizen Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="Q426">[https://www.osaka-city-callcenter.jp/faq/detail.aspx?id=426 Q. What are the summer festivals in Osaka City?] Osaka City Comprehensive Call Center (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> In some cases, it refers to the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and {{ill|Ikutama Festival|ja|生國魂祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutama Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="tennoji17">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000404/404034/tennoji17-07_p02.pdf Tennōji Ward Newsletter, No. 254, July 1, 2017] Tennōji Ward (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>
== Explanation of Each Festival ==
The three festivals consist of the Tenjin Festival of [[:en:Osaka Tenmangū|Osaka Tenmangu Shrine]] in [[:en:Kita-ku, Osaka|Kita-ku]], the Sumiyoshi Festival of [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], and the Aizen Festival at the [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitennōji Temple]] branch Aizen-dō (Araryōzan Shōman'in) in [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennōji-ku]]. These are sometimes collectively referred to as Osaka’s Three Great Summer Festivals.<ref name="Q426" /><ref name="osaka2">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000241/241118/15041934_2.pdf JR Momodani Station] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> They are also called Naniwa’s Three Great Summer Festivals.
Due to their common nicknames—Aizen-san, Tenjin-san, and Sumiyoshi-san—there is a saying, "Osaka’s summer festivals begin with Aizen-san and end with Sumiyoshi-san." This saying is thought to be the origin of the phrase "ai (愛, love) sumi (住, live) masen (ません, sorry)," or as a mnemonic, "ai (愛) sumi (住) masen (天, Tenjin)," representing the three festivals.
In some cases, the term includes the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and the Ikutama Festival of [[:en:Ikukunitama Shrine|Ikukunitama Shrine]] in Tennōji-ku, which is considered a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi" />
=== Tenjin Festival === The Tenjin Festival is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本三大一覧|1=Japan's Top 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10311536}}.<ref name="Q426" />
It typically begins with the eve of the festival on July 24, followed by the land procession and boat procession on the 25th. The boat procession features a grand fleet of 100 boats magnificently navigating the Okawa River.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Sumiyoshi Festival === The Sumiyoshi Festival usually begins with the eve festival on July 30, followed by the main festival and the Nagoshi Harae (Summer Purification Ritual) on the 31st, and concludes with the Mikoshi Procession on August 1.<ref name="Q426" /> The Nagoshi Harae is famous for the ritual of passing through a ring of straw (chinowa-kuguri) and is designated as an intangible cultural asset of Osaka Prefecture.<ref name="Q426" /> The Mikoshi Procession involves carrying the portable shrine from Sumiyoshi Taisha to Shukuin Tongū in Sakai City.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Aizen Festival === The Aizen Festival is held annually from June 30 to July 2 and is also known as the "Yukata Festival."<ref name="Q426" />
=== Ikutama Festival === The Ikutama Festival of Ikutama Shrine (held annually from July 11 to 12) is sometimes included. While the Tenjin Festival is referred to as the "River Tenjin" due to its boat procession, the Ikutama Festival is called the "Land Ikutama" and features a land procession, making it a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/chuo/cmsfiles/contents/0000508/508145/nabi2ura.pdf Connecting People, Cities, and History Through Festivals: Shinno-san and Ikutama-san Festivals and Traditions] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大阪三大夏祭り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11440018]] Wikidata name: Three Great Summer Festivals of Osaka [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Osaka's Three Great Summer Festivals''' (大阪三大夏祭り) are the three representative summer festivals held in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[:en:Osaka City|City]]: [[:en:Tenjin Matsuri|Tenjin Festival]], {{ill|Sumiyoshi Festival|ja|住吉祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Aizen Festival|ja|愛染まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizen Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="Q426">[https://www.osaka-city-callcenter.jp/faq/detail.aspx?id=426 Q. What are the summer festivals in Osaka City?] Osaka City Comprehensive Call Center (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> In some cases, it refers to the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and {{ill|Ikutama Festival|ja|生國魂祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutama Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="tennoji17">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000404/404034/tennoji17-07_p02.pdf Tennōji Ward Newsletter, No. 254, July 1, 2017] Tennōji Ward (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>
== Explanation of Each Festival ==
The three festivals consist of the Tenjin Festival of [[:en:Osaka Tenmangū|Osaka Tenmangu Shrine]] in [[:en:Kita-ku, Osaka|Kita-ku]], the Sumiyoshi Festival of [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], and the Aizen Festival at the [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitennōji Temple]] branch Aizen-dō (Araryōzan Shōman'in) in [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennōji-ku]]. These are sometimes collectively referred to as Osaka’s Three Great Summer Festivals.<ref name="Q426" /><ref name="osaka2">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000241/241118/15041934_2.pdf JR Momodani Station] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> They are also called Naniwa’s Three Great Summer Festivals.
Due to their common nicknames—Aizen-san, Tenjin-san, and Sumiyoshi-san—there is a saying, "Osaka’s summer festivals begin with Aizen-san and end with Sumiyoshi-san." This saying is thought to be the origin of the phrase "ai (愛, love) sumi (住, live) masen (ません, sorry)," or as a mnemonic, "ai (愛) sumi (住) masen (天, Tenjin)," representing the three festivals.
In some cases, the term includes the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and the Ikutama Festival of [[:en:Ikukunitama Shrine|Ikukunitama Shrine]] in Tennōji-ku, which is considered a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi" />
=== Tenjin Festival === The Tenjin Festival is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本三大一覧|1=Japan's Top 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10311536}}.<ref name="Q426" />
It typically begins with the eve of the festival on July 24, followed by the land procession and boat procession on the 25th. The boat procession features a grand fleet of 100 boats magnificently navigating the Okawa River.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Sumiyoshi Festival === The Sumiyoshi Festival usually begins with the eve festival on July 30, followed by the main festival and the Nagoshi Harae (Summer Purification Ritual) on the 31st, and concludes with the Mikoshi Procession on August 1.<ref name="Q426" /> The Nagoshi Harae is famous for the ritual of passing through a ring of straw (chinowa-kuguri) and is designated as an intangible cultural asset of Osaka Prefecture.<ref name="Q426" /> The Mikoshi Procession involves carrying the portable shrine from Sumiyoshi Taisha to Shukuin Tongū in Sakai City.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Aizen Festival === The Aizen Festival is held annually from June 30 to July 2 and is also known as the "Yukata Festival."<ref name="Q426" />
=== Ikutama Festival === The Ikutama Festival of Ikutama Shrine (held annually from July 11 to 12) is sometimes included. While the Tenjin Festival is referred to as the "River Tenjin" due to its boat procession, the Ikutama Festival is called the "Land Ikutama" and features a land procession, making it a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/chuo/cmsfiles/contents/0000508/508145/nabi2ura.pdf Connecting People, Cities, and History Through Festivals: Shinno-san and Ikutama-san Festivals and Traditions] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大阪三大夏祭り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11440018]] Wikidata name: Three Great Summer Festivals of Osaka [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Osaka's Three Great Summer Festivals''' (大阪三大夏祭り) are the three representative summer festivals held in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[:en:Osaka City|City]]: [[:en:Tenjin Matsuri|Tenjin Festival]], {{ill|Sumiyoshi Festival|ja|住吉祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Aizen Festival|ja|愛染まつり|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizen Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="Q426">[https://www.osaka-city-callcenter.jp/faq/detail.aspx?id=426 Q. What are the summer festivals in Osaka City?] Osaka City Comprehensive Call Center (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> In some cases, it refers to the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and {{ill|Ikutama Festival|ja|生國魂祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutama Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="tennoji17">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000404/404034/tennoji17-07_p02.pdf Tennōji Ward Newsletter, No. 254, July 1, 2017] Tennōji Ward (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>
== Explanation of Each Festival ==
The three festivals consist of the Tenjin Festival of [[:en:Osaka Tenmangū|Osaka Tenmangu Shrine]] in [[:en:Kita-ku, Osaka|Kita-ku]], the Sumiyoshi Festival of [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], and the Aizen Festival at the [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitennōji Temple]] branch Aizen-dō (Araryōzan Shōman'in) in [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennōji-ku]]. These are sometimes collectively referred to as Osaka’s Three Great Summer Festivals.<ref name="Q426" /><ref name="osaka2">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000241/241118/15041934_2.pdf JR Momodani Station] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> They are also called Naniwa’s Three Great Summer Festivals.
Due to their common nicknames—Aizen-san, Tenjin-san, and Sumiyoshi-san—there is a saying, "Osaka’s summer festivals begin with Aizen-san and end with Sumiyoshi-san." This saying is thought to be the origin of the phrase "ai (愛, love) sumi (住, live) masen (ません, sorry)," or as a mnemonic, "ai (愛) sumi (住) masen (天, Tenjin)," representing the three festivals.
In some cases, the term includes the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and the Ikutama Festival of [[:en:Ikukunitama Shrine|Ikukunitama Shrine]] in Tennōji-ku, which is considered a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi" />
=== Tenjin Festival === The Tenjin Festival is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大祭り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本三大一覧|1=Japan's Top 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10311536}}.<ref name="Q426" />
It typically begins with the eve of the festival on July 24, followed by the land procession and boat procession on the 25th. The boat procession features a grand fleet of 100 boats magnificently navigating the Okawa River.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Sumiyoshi Festival === The Sumiyoshi Festival usually begins with the eve festival on July 30, followed by the main festival and the Nagoshi Harae (Summer Purification Ritual) on the 31st, and concludes with the Mikoshi Procession on August 1.<ref name="Q426" /> The Nagoshi Harae is famous for the ritual of passing through a ring of straw (chinowa-kuguri) and is designated as an intangible cultural asset of Osaka Prefecture.<ref name="Q426" /> The Mikoshi Procession involves carrying the portable shrine from Sumiyoshi Taisha to Shukuin Tongū in Sakai City.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Aizen Festival === The Aizen Festival is held annually from June 30 to July 2 and is also known as the "Yukata Festival."<ref name="Q426" />
=== Ikutama Festival === The Ikutama Festival of Ikutama Shrine (held annually from July 11 to 12) is sometimes included. While the Tenjin Festival is referred to as the "River Tenjin" due to its boat procession, the Ikutama Festival is called the "Land Ikutama" and features a land procession, making it a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/chuo/cmsfiles/contents/0000508/508145/nabi2ura.pdf Connecting People, Cities, and History Through Festivals: Shinno-san and Ikutama-san Festivals and Traditions] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大阪三大夏祭り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11440018]] Wikidata name: Three Great Summer Festivals of Osaka [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Osaka's Three Great Summer Festivals''' (大阪三大夏祭り) are the three representative summer festivals held in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[:en:Osaka City|City]]: [[:en:Tenjin Matsuri|Tenjin Festival]], {{ill|Sumiyoshi Festival|ja|住吉祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Aizen Festival|ja|愛染まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizen Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="Q426">[https://www.osaka-city-callcenter.jp/faq/detail.aspx?id=426 Q. What are the summer festivals in Osaka City?] Osaka City Comprehensive Call Center (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> In some cases, it refers to the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and {{ill|Ikutama Festival|ja|生國魂祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutama Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="tennoji17">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000404/404034/tennoji17-07_p02.pdf Tennōji Ward Newsletter, No. 254, July 1, 2017] Tennōji Ward (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>
== Explanation of Each Festival ==
The three festivals consist of the Tenjin Festival of [[:en:Osaka Tenmangū|Osaka Tenmangu Shrine]] in [[:en:Kita-ku, Osaka|Kita-ku]], the Sumiyoshi Festival of [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], and the Aizen Festival at the [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitennōji Temple]] branch Aizen-dō (Araryōzan Shōman'in) in [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennōji-ku]]. These are sometimes collectively referred to as Osaka’s Three Great Summer Festivals.<ref name="Q426" /><ref name="osaka2">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000241/241118/15041934_2.pdf JR Momodani Station] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> They are also called Naniwa’s Three Great Summer Festivals.
Due to their common nicknames—Aizen-san, Tenjin-san, and Sumiyoshi-san—there is a saying, "Osaka’s summer festivals begin with Aizen-san and end with Sumiyoshi-san." This saying is thought to be the origin of the phrase "ai (愛, love) sumi (住, live) masen (ません, sorry)," or as a mnemonic, "ai (愛) sumi (住) masen (天, Tenjin)," representing the three festivals.
In some cases, the term includes the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and the Ikutama Festival of [[:en:Ikukunitama Shrine|Ikukunitama Shrine]] in Tennōji-ku, which is considered a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi" />
=== Tenjin Festival === The Tenjin Festival is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本三大一覧|1=Japan's Top 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10311536}}.<ref name="Q426" />
It typically begins with the eve of the festival on July 24, followed by the land procession and boat procession on the 25th. The boat procession features a grand fleet of 100 boats magnificently navigating the Okawa River.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Sumiyoshi Festival === The Sumiyoshi Festival usually begins with the eve festival on July 30, followed by the main festival and the Nagoshi Harae (Summer Purification Ritual) on the 31st, and concludes with the Mikoshi Procession on August 1.<ref name="Q426" /> The Nagoshi Harae is famous for the ritual of passing through a ring of straw (chinowa-kuguri) and is designated as an intangible cultural asset of Osaka Prefecture.<ref name="Q426" /> The Mikoshi Procession involves carrying the portable shrine from Sumiyoshi Taisha to Shukuin Tongū in Sakai City.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Aizen Festival === The Aizen Festival is held annually from June 30 to July 2 and is also known as the "Yukata Festival."<ref name="Q426" />
=== Ikutama Festival === The Ikutama Festival of Ikutama Shrine (held annually from July 11 to 12) is sometimes included. While the Tenjin Festival is referred to as the "River Tenjin" due to its boat procession, the Ikutama Festival is called the "Land Ikutama" and features a land procession, making it a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/chuo/cmsfiles/contents/0000508/508145/nabi2ura.pdf Connecting People, Cities, and History Through Festivals: Shinno-san and Ikutama-san Festivals and Traditions] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>

* {{ill|Spring Matsuri|lt=Spring Matsuri|lt=Spring Festivals|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Autumn Matsuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Winter Matsuri|lt=Winter Matsuri|lt=Winter Festivals|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Winter Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Tanabata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabata|qq=}} * {{ill|Community building|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Community building|qq=}} * {{ill|Homecoming|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homecoming|qq=}} * {{ill|Summer Vacation|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Vacation|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大阪三大夏祭り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11440018]] Wikidata name: Three Great Summer Festivals of Osaka [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Osaka's Three Great Summer Festivals''' (大阪三大夏祭り) are the three representative summer festivals held in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[:en:Osaka City|City]]: [[:en:Tenjin Matsuri|Tenjin Festival]], {{ill|Sumiyoshi Festival|ja|住吉祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Aizen Festival|ja|愛染まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizen Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="Q426">[https://www.osaka-city-callcenter.jp/faq/detail.aspx?id=426 Q. What are the summer festivals in Osaka City?] Osaka City Comprehensive Call Center (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> In some cases, it refers to the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and {{ill|Ikutama Festival|ja|生國魂祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutama Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="tennoji17">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000404/404034/tennoji17-07_p02.pdf Tennōji Ward Newsletter, No. 254, July 1, 2017] Tennōji Ward (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>
== Explanation of Each Festival ==
The three festivals consist of the Tenjin Festival of [[:en:Osaka Tenmangū|Osaka Tenmangu Shrine]] in [[:en:Kita-ku, Osaka|Kita-ku]], the Sumiyoshi Festival of [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], and the Aizen Festival at the [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitennōji Temple]] branch Aizen-dō (Araryōzan Shōman'in) in [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennōji-ku]]. These are sometimes collectively referred to as Osaka’s Three Great Summer Festivals.<ref name="Q426" /><ref name="osaka2">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000241/241118/15041934_2.pdf JR Momodani Station] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref> They are also called Naniwa’s Three Great Summer Festivals.
Due to their common nicknames—Aizen-san, Tenjin-san, and Sumiyoshi-san—there is a saying, "Osaka’s summer festivals begin with Aizen-san and end with Sumiyoshi-san." This saying is thought to be the origin of the phrase "ai (愛, love) sumi (住, live) masen (ません, sorry)," or as a mnemonic, "ai (愛) sumi (住) masen (天, Tenjin)," representing the three festivals.
In some cases, the term includes the Tenjin Festival, Sumiyoshi Festival, and the Ikutama Festival of [[:en:Ikukunitama Shrine|Ikukunitama Shrine]] in Tennōji-ku, which is considered a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi" />
=== Tenjin Festival === The Tenjin Festival is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本三大一覧|1=Japan's Top 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10311536}}.<ref name="Q426" />
It typically begins with the eve of the festival on July 24, followed by the land procession and boat procession on the 25th. The boat procession features a grand fleet of 100 boats magnificently navigating the Okawa River.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Sumiyoshi Festival === The Sumiyoshi Festival usually begins with the eve festival on July 30, followed by the main festival and the Nagoshi Harae (Summer Purification Ritual) on the 31st, and concludes with the Mikoshi Procession on August 1.<ref name="Q426" /> The Nagoshi Harae is famous for the ritual of passing through a ring of straw (chinowa-kuguri) and is designated as an intangible cultural asset of Osaka Prefecture.<ref name="Q426" /> The Mikoshi Procession involves carrying the portable shrine from Sumiyoshi Taisha to Shukuin Tongū in Sakai City.<ref name="Q426" />
=== Aizen Festival === The Aizen Festival is held annually from June 30 to July 2 and is also known as the "Yukata Festival."<ref name="Q426" />
=== Ikutama Festival === The Ikutama Festival of Ikutama Shrine (held annually from July 11 to 12) is sometimes included. While the Tenjin Festival is referred to as the "River Tenjin" due to its boat procession, the Ikutama Festival is called the "Land Ikutama" and features a land procession, making it a counterpart to the Tenjin Festival.<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="nabi">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/chuo/cmsfiles/contents/0000508/508145/nabi2ura.pdf Connecting People, Cities, and History Through Festivals: Shinno-san and Ikutama-san Festivals and Traditions] Osaka City (Accessed July 15, 2021)</ref>
